last_week_candidates.summary: HIRING: AI Automation Specialist (Full-Time) Details about the position: - $1000 to $2000 USD per month! We are open to paying more than $2000 USD per month, depending on your skill level & experience. - Work from home! - 9AM to 6PM Eastern Time - Monday to...
last_week_candidates.full_description: HIRING: AI Automation Specialist (Full-Time) Details about the position: - $1000 to $2000 USD per month! We are open to paying more than $2000 USD per month, depending on your skill level & experience. - Work from home! - 9AM to 6PM Eastern Time - Monday to Friday! ------------------------ REQUIREMENTS - Fully fluent in English, both spoken and written. - Previous experience building automations using Make, Zapier or n8n. - Previous experience using an AI coding agent such as Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, etc. You should be comfortable using coding agents to work on repos inside GitHub, make changes, troubleshoot issues, and confirm that the final result works correctly. A significant part of this role will involve using coding agents to build and improve reusable skills, instructions, and workflows that will be used by AI agents. - Comfortable working with APIs, webhooks, JSON, authentication, basic scripts, and AI-agent tools such as MCP servers. You do not need to be an expert in every term listed above. The more you understand, the better, but we can teach specific tools if you have strong fundamentals. We do not require you to know how to code, but it is a bonus. You do not need to be a professional software developer, but you should understand how different tools communicate with each other and be able to troubleshoot integrations when something goes wrong. Preferred, but not required: - Previous programming or software-development experience - Experience writing or modifying scripts - Experience with Google Sheets, Google Forms, Google Drive, Airtable, ClickUp, or GoHighLevel - Experience with AI image and video tools such as GPT Image, Kling, Seedance, Grok Imagine, or similar models - Experience with agents such as Hermes and OpenClaw You do not need experience with every tool we use. We can teach you our specific systems and workflows, but you must already have hands-on experience with AI coding agents, automation platforms, and AI-powered workflows. ------------------------ MAIN RESPONSIBILITIES Your main responsibility would be to automate repetitive processes in the company using mainly AI agents, and some Make/Zapier automations as well. Common examples might look like: - Building and improving AI-agent skills, instructions, and workflows. - Automating the generation of AI ads using image/video AI models. - Automating the onboarding of new clients. - Automating some of our copywriter's tasks. - Automating the tracking of all our data. We already have A LOT of automations + AI workflows set up - you'd be coming into an already existing system. Your goal would be to improve it and keep it up to date. ------------------------ COMPANY INFORMATION We are a marketing company that specializes in Meta Ads (among other things) You would help us automate tasks and processes so that we can create more ads for ourselves and our clients! Last year, we generated over 35,000 leads - you will be contributing to our efforts this year, where we aim to break this goal! ------------------------ So if you're someone who's: 1 - An overachiever 2 - Detail-oriented 3 - Works hard 4 - Proactive 5 - Resourceful Please apply using this link: b2bscale.pro/automation-specialist-hiring-ojph When asked what your favorite color is in the application, please put Magenta as your answer, or else you will be disqualified. Do not send us a message here on Onlinejobs - we do not look at messages. You need to apply using that link and fill out the 5-minute application form. Thank you!

last_week_candidates.title: Jr Vibe Coder / Developer Wanted
last_week_candidates.summary: This Isn't a Job. It's a Lifestyle. You know who you are. You're the person who stays up until 3am building something nobody asked you to build - just because you wanted to see if it would work. You've got 14 browser tabs open right now, half of them are AI...
last_week_candidates.full_description: This Isn't a Job. It's a Lifestyle. You know who you are. You're the person who stays up until 3am building something nobody asked you to build - just because you wanted to see if it would work. You've got 14 browser tabs open right now, half of them are AI tools you're testing. You saw Claude Code drop a new feature yesterday and you already tried it. You read Hacker News, you follow AI Twitter, you've got a Discord server where people share prompts. You don't just USE technology - you chase it, you break it, you figure out how it works, and you build things with it. You're not looking for a 9-to-5 where someone hands you a ticket and you close it. You want to be in the trenches with someone who moves as fast as you do - building landing pages, spinning up websites, ripping competitor funnels and making them better, wiring up tracking, deploying to production, fixing things that break at midnight because you WANT to, not because someone told you to. We're a digital marketing company that builds fast and ships daily. We use AI coding tools - Claude Code, Codex, Cursor - as our primary workflow. We build things in hours that used to take weeks. And we need a junior tech who can keep up. ## What You'll Actually Do - Build and deploy landing pages from scratch or by ripping and rebuilding existing ones - FULLY WORKING means Google Ads tracking, Facebook Pixel, page speed optimized, above-the-fold perfect, mobile-tested, QA'd before handoff - Implement ad tracking: Facebook Pixel, Google Ads conversion tags, GTM, server-side tagging - Optimize page speed (Core Web Vitals, Lighthouse scores matter here) - Build and customize Shopify stores - Handle system admin - DNS, SSL, hosting, Cloudflare, cPanel, Linux servers - Integrate APIs, webhooks, tracking pixels, conversion APIs - Build full websites and web tools - QA your own work before anyone sees it - you catch the bugs, not us - Live in the CLI/terminal - Git, deployment, server management - Use AI coding tools as your daily driver - not as a novelty ## Who You Are - A tech nerd. Computers aren't your job, they're your life. You code as a hobby. You build side projects for fun. - An AI wizard who's already deep in the game - Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Windsurf, whatever dropped last week. You keep up because you can't help it. - A problem solver. When something breaks, you don't panic and you don't ask "what do I do?" - you dig in and figure it out. - Fast. Not sloppy-fast, but "I understand the tools well enough to move at speed without cutting corners" fast. - Self-directed. You get a brief, you ship the result. You don't need someone standing over your shoulder. - Young, hungry, and ready to grind. We work hard and we expect you to match that energy. ## You Must Have - Real, daily experience with AI coding tools (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Windsurf, or similar) - this is non-negotiable - Solid HTML, CSS, JavaScript fundamentals - you understand what AI generates and can debug it when it's wrong - Experience building and deploying landing pages or websites - Knowledge of ad tracking (Facebook Pixel, Google Ads tags, GTM) - CLI/terminal comfort - you live in the command line - Problem-solving ability - you don't wait to be told every step - Professional English communication ## Bonus Points - Shopify development (Liquid, theme customization) - Cloudflare Pages/Workers - Server-side tracking (CAPI, sGTM) - WordPress/PHP - Python or Node.js - Page speed optimization expertise - Tracking platforms (ClickFlare, Voluum) - You follow AI news daily and can tell us what's new THIS WEEK ## The Deal $500/mo to start. We're not going to pretend - this is entry-level pay for an entry-level position. But here's what we WILL do: if you're a superstar - if you ship fast, solve problems, and prove you're the real deal - your pay grows. Fast. We don't keep good people at starter pay. We reward results, not resumes. You'll work directly with the owner. No middle management, no bureaucracy. Async communication (chat/messages), not pointless meetings. You'll learn more here in 3 months than most people learn in a year because you'll be building real things that make real money, not doing tutorials. If this sounds like work to you, this isn't your role. If this sounds like Tuesday, apply now. ## Application Questions Answer ALL of these. Real answers, not AI-generated fluff. If you use AI to write your application for an AI coding role, we'll know - and that tells us you don't understand the difference between using AI as a tool and letting AI replace you. 1. What AI coding tools do you use? Describe your actual workflow - how do you go from a task to a shipped result using AI? 2. Share a link to something you built using AI coding tools (landing page, website, Shopify store, app, tool - anything). What was it, and how long did it take? 3. If I said "rip this lander and make it work" - walk me through exactly what you'd do, step by step. Include tracking, page speed, and QA. 4. What's your experience with ad tracking? (Facebook Pixel, Google Ads tags, GTM, server-side tracking - be specific about what you've actually set up) 5. Tell me about a technical problem you solved on your own. What broke, what did you try, how did you fix it? 6. What CLI/terminal tools do you use? Do you use Git? How do you deploy? 7. Rate yourself 1-10 on each (be honest - I'll check): HTML/CSS, JavaScript, Shopify, Linux/server admin, page speed optimization, API integrations. 8. What's the coolest AI tool or feature you discovered in the last 30 days? What did you do with it? 9. How do you QA your own work before handing it off? Walk me through your process. 10. When can you start? Are you available full-time?

last_week_candidates.summary: AI First. If you didn't start reading this ad already thinking about how you'd automate it — this role probably isn't for you. But if you live and breathe AI, keep reading. We're SnappyVAS (snappyvas.com) — an AI-first company and a no-code platform for...
last_week_candidates.full_description: AI First. If you didn't start reading this ad already thinking about how you'd automate it — this role probably isn't for you. But if you live and breathe AI, keep reading. We're SnappyVAS (snappyvas.com) — an AI-first company and a no-code platform for building and deploying AI agents: chat widgets, landing pages, bookings, lead qualification, and multi-channel deploy to Slack, WhatsApp, Telegram, Discord, and Lark. We practice what we preach: we run our own operations on AI, and we're hiring a right-hand VA who does the same. This is NOT a traditional back-office VA job. We don't want someone who waits for instructions and copies data between spreadsheets. This is a growth-and-promotion role. You'll work directly with the SnappyVAS directors, you'll work ON the platform every day — using it, testing new features, and promoting it — and you'll go find businesses that need us and help bring them in. We want someone smart and hungry — a builder obsessed with productivity, automation, and finding smarter ways to work using AI every single day. If you're the kind of person who teaches yourself new tools on the weekend, gets a kick out of automating things away, and loves the hustle of putting a product in front of the right people, you'll fit right in. The AI tools you must know (in order of importance) Claude Code / Cowork — this is essential and non-negotiable Lovable ChatGPT Perplexity If you're constantly building, researching, automating, promoting, and solving problems with these tools, we want to hear from you. What you'll do Support the directors — act as a right hand to the SnappyVAS founders, handling research, communications, follow-ups, and whatever moves the business forward Work ON the platform — use SnappyVAS daily, build and test agents, stress-test new features, and report back what's great and what needs fixing Market and promote SnappyVAS — create content, outreach, and campaigns that get the platform in front of the businesses who need it Business development & lead generation — research and find prospects (trades, local service businesses, restaurants, creators, and small businesses), build target lists, and help bring them in as customers Automate the growth engine — build AI-driven workflows in Claude Code/Cowork for prospecting, outreach, and reporting; find repetitive work and automate it away Build with Lovable — help ship landing pages, demos, and internal tools Write advanced prompts that produce high-quality, reliable results Research and test new AI tools, then put the best ones to work Create SOPs, documentation, and reports that keep the operation sharp What you need Extensive hands-on experience with Claude Code/Cowork (required), Lovable, ChatGPT, and Perplexity Excellent prompt engineering skills Strong research and lead-generation instincts — you can find the right businesses and the right people to contact A promoter's mindset — comfortable with outreach, marketing, and putting a product in front of people Highly organised with exceptional attention to detail Self-motivated and proactive — a builder who finds solutions instead of waiting to be told Excellent written and spoken English Reliable internet connection and a proper home office setup Bonus points Automation tools: n8n, Make, or Zapier Basic coding or API knowledge Experience in sales, outreach, marketing, or social media Experience supporting business owners, founders, or executives What we offer Fully remote, work-from-home A front-row seat with the founders and real influence over how we grow Long-term opportunity with real career growth An AI-first company that adopts the latest tech constantly Exciting work across many industries as we bring new businesses onto the platform How to Apply To apply, please send: Your resume A short video introducing yourself A summary of your experience with Claude Code/Cowork, Lovable, ChatGPT, and Perplexity Examples of AI workflows, prompts, or projects you've built Which AI tool you use the most, and why Application Test (read carefully) Start your application with the words "AI First" and tell us about the most impressive thing you've built or automated using Claude Code/Cowork. Applications that do not follow these instructions will not be considered. Shortlisted applicants will be invited to complete a short online skills exam. Note: the exam must be completed on your own — using AI to answer the questions will disqualify you (more on that when you're invited). Study snappyvas.com for platform questionsRewrite job ad for onlinejobs.phDesign 20-question screening exam + prerequisitesBuild screening app live in Lovable5Adding auto-email of resultsSnappyVAS_OnlineJobsPH_Ad.mdSnappyVAS_Screening_Exam.mdTrack tools and referenced files used in this task.

last_week_candidates.title: AI Automation Specialist / Executive Operations Assistant
last_week_candidates.summary: I am hiring a full-time AI Automation Specialist to help run and improve a fast-moving sales and lead generation operation. This is not a basic VA role. I need someone who is highly experienced with AI tools, automation tools, CRM systems, and workflow...
last_week_candidates.full_description: I am hiring a full-time AI Automation Specialist to help run and improve a fast-moving sales and lead generation operation. This is not a basic VA role. I need someone who is highly experienced with AI tools, automation tools, CRM systems, and workflow building. You must be able to use AI to move faster, organize systems, automate repetitive work, improve processes, create workflows, and support daily sales operations. Required tools include: - Claude - ChatGPT - Perplexity - Codex - GoHighLevel - Facebook Meta Business Suite - Google Workspace - Automation tools such as Zapier, Make, or similar platforms Perplexity, Claude, ChatGPT, GoHighLevel, and Meta experience are mandatory. If you do not use AI tools daily, do not apply. WHAT YOU WILL DO You will help with: - AI-assisted workflow creation - CRM organization and automation - GoHighLevel pipelines and follow-up systems - Meta Business Suite support - Facebook and Instagram lead management - Lead tracking and reporting - Automation of repetitive tasks - Appointment setting support - Google Sheets / Drive organization - Daily KPI reports - Process improvement - Sales support systems - Creating scripts, templates, and operational workflows You will be expected to think like an operator, not just an assistant. If a task is repetitive, I expect you to find a way to make it faster, cleaner, or automated. REQUIRED EXPERIENCE You must have real experience with: - Claude - ChatGPT - Perplexity - GoHighLevel - Facebook Meta Business Suite - Google Sheets - Google Drive - CRM systems - Workflow automation - Lead generation or sales support - Daily reporting Preferred experience: - Codex - Zapier - Make - WhatsApp Business - Facebook Marketplace - Craigslist - Meta Ads Manager - Appointment setting - Automotive sales support MUST HAVE - Strong English - Fast typing - Reliable internet - Ability to work 40 hours per week - Ability to clock in and out - Comfortable with screen tracking during work hours - Strong attention to detail - Ability to follow instructions exactly - Ability to think independently - Ability to use AI tools to work faster - Honesty about your actual skill level IMPORTANT This is a full-time production role. Time compliance is non-negotiable. Screen tracking is required. Daily reports are required. You must be comfortable being measured by activity, accuracy, and results. Do not apply if you are only a basic VA. Do not apply if you are not strong with AI tools. Do not apply if you are exaggerating your automation experience. COMPENSATION $400/month during training/probation. Commission available after training based on appointments, shows, and completed results. This role can earn more if you produce. HOW TO APPLY Use the subject line: AI AUTOMATION SPECIALIST Answer every question below in order: 1. How many hours per week can you work? 2. Are you comfortable with clock-in/clock-out and screen monitoring? 3. How do you use ChatGPT in your daily workflow? 4. How do you use Claude in your daily workflow? 5. How do you use Perplexity in your daily workflow? 6. Have you used Codex or any AI coding/workflow tool? Explain. 7. Have you used GoHighLevel? What exactly did you build or manage? 8. Have you used Facebook Meta Business Suite? What did you manage? 9. What automation tools have you used? Zapier, Make, Power Automate, or others? 10. Give one specific example of a workflow you automated. 11. Have you done lead generation or appointment setting before? 12. What tools are you strongest with? 13. What tools do you still need training on? 14. Are you willing to complete a paid test task before being hired full-time? FINAL NOTE I am looking for someone sharp, serious, AI-capable, and automation-minded. The right person will help build systems, speed up workflows, organize leads, and make the operation faster every week. If you are excellent with AI tools, automation, CRM systems, and execution, this can become a strong long-term opportunity.

You will work directly with our leadership team to design and build agentic AI systems that automate real business processes, surface insights, and extend the capability of our team across every function. You are not writing specs — you are shipping code that matters. The immediate goal is to build out Buildmetric's company-wide agentic infrastructure using OpenClaw and Claude — the workflows, dashboards, and automation systems that will transform how we operate, from estimating and client management through to reporting, SOPs, and internal processes. This is foundational work with real and lasting impact. Key Responsibilities - Agentic AI workflows using OpenClaw and the Anthropic Claude API — multi-step agents that research, reason, retrieve, and act autonomously across our business - Internal dashboards and operational tools built on OpenClaw that surface business-critical data in real time and give our team full visibility - MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers and plugin systems that extend Claude and OpenClaw into our data sources, tools, and third-party platforms - API integrations connecting our systems with CRMs, accounting software, project management platforms, estimating tools, and more - Company-wide automation pipelines that eliminate manual, repetitive tasks across estimating, reporting, client management, and internal operations - SOPs and workflow documentation systems — helping define, codify, and automate our operating procedures as we scale What We’re Looking For - Hands-on experience with OpenClaw — this is our current dashboard platform and the foundation for our agentic build-out - Solid experience with the Anthropic Claude API, Claude Code, and the Claude Agent SDK - Genuinely current with the AI landscape — you know what is shipping this week, not just what shipped last year - Strong JavaScript/TypeScript or Python (ideally both) — production-quality code, not just demos or prototypes - Comfortable building REST APIs, webhooks, and integrating third-party services end to end - Solid grasp of agentic patterns: tool calling, multi-step reasoning, memory and context management, agent orchestration - Ability to understand business workflows, processes, and operating systems — you can translate how a business actually works into clean automation logic - Works independently, self-manages effectively, and communicates clearly in English Who You Are - Young, sharp, and ambitious — you care deeply about doing excellent work, not just completing tasks - You experiment constantly. You have already built things with AI tools on your own time because you wanted to, not because you were told to - You understand business — you can look at how a company operates and identify where AI and automation creates real leverage - You communicate proactively, flag problems early, and ask the right questions before going down the wrong path -You take full ownership of what you build, from design through deployment and ongoing iteration - You thrive when the technology is evolving fast and the target moves — that excites you rather than stresses you How to Apply Send us an email with the subject line "Agentic AI Developer — [Your Name]". Tell us: - A short intro: who you are, where you are based, and why this role excites you - Links to things you have actually built — GitHub repos, live demos, agents, side projects, anything that shows how you think and build - Specific notes on your OpenClaw and Claude / Anthropic API experience - Your availability and earliest possible start date Apply to: karen@buildmetric.com.au We read every application. Shortlisted candidates will hear from us quickly. No recruiters or agencies, please.

last_week_candidates.full_description: Full Stack Software Engineer (Python, Automation & AI Systems) About Us We are looking for an experienced Full Stack Software Engineer with strong software engineering fundamentals and hands-on experience building production applications. We actively use AI-assisted development tools and expect candidates to do the same. However, we are not looking for vibe coders. We are looking for engineers who understand software architecture, security, databases, APIs, scalability, deployment, and multi-tenant systems, and who can confidently design, build, debug, deploy, and maintain production software. The ideal candidate uses AI to accelerate development and improve productivity while applying sound engineering judgment and real-world technical expertise to build secure, scalable, and reliable systems. Computer Science, Software Engineering, or equivalent professional experience is strongly preferred. What You'll Do • Build and maintain web applications, SaaS platforms, internal business tools, and automation systems • Create API integrations and AI-powered workflows • Design, troubleshoot, optimize, and scale production systems • Research and implement emerging AI technologies and development tools • Collaborate on software, e-commerce, automation, and business projects • Work during New York business hours Required Technical Skills Core Development: Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, React, Next.js, Node.js, FastAPI, Django, Express, or similar modern frameworks. Software Engineering: Strong understanding of software architecture, APIs, authentication, security, databases, scalability, deployment, and multi-tenant application design. Databases & Cloud: PostgreSQL, Supabase, MySQL, Neon, AWS, Cloudflare, Vercel, Azure, Google Cloud, or similar platforms. Experience integrating LLM APIs, AI workflows, agents, RAG systems, vector databases, and AI-powered applications. Nice To Have • Shopify development • Stripe integrations • Playwright, Selenium, browser automation, or web scraping • Docker, Kubernetes, DevOps, and CI/CD • SaaS, marketplace, e-commerce, or multi-tenant platform experience To Apply Please send: • Resume and portfolio • Examples of recent projects • A short description of the most impressive software system, automation platform, or application you have built • The AI tools you currently use in your workflow Attention to detail is very important, when applying, please use the following subject line, otherwise we won't view you request: "Full Stack Software & AI Engineer 6172026”.

last_week_candidates.title: AI Developer
last_week_candidates.summary: We're looking for an AI-first developer who genuinely loves building and shows up with the plan. If you light up when you talk about wiring agents together, shipping internal tools fast, and using AI as a daily working partner, and you'd rather drive the...
last_week_candidates.full_description: We're looking for an AI-first developer who genuinely loves building and shows up with the plan. If you light up when you talk about wiring agents together, shipping internal tools fast, and using AI as a daily working partner, and you'd rather drive the build strategy than wait for a spec, we'd love to hear from you. The Role This is a gig-based, project-driven build role focused on internal tooling. Here's what you'd actually be doing: - Building agents and automations from the ground up, including wiring up MCP servers and Claude Code workflows - Building a range of internal tools for the team, the kind of things that remove manual work and speed up how we operate - Working across APIs and integrations, including GoHighLevel, to connect the systems we run on - Partnering with the team on what gets built and why. We work as a team to win, and you'd own the technical thinking on your side of it Within the first 30 to 60 days, success looks like you showing up with the plan. You should be telling us what we should build next, how to approach it, and in what order. You should have a clear point of view on what's worth automating, what isn't, and what you want to ship first. The bar here is strategic ownership of the build, not waiting for a backlog to be handed to you. Schedule Work hours are flexible but need to overlap with our team between 9:00 AM and 5:00 PM EST during active project work. Just want to make sure you can meet inside that window. What We're Looking For - An AI-first mindset. You use AI as a real working partner every day, and it shows in how you work. - Independent problem-solving. You think first, use AI to amplify your thinking, and verify before shipping anything. - A strategy-first approach. You're someone who shows up with the plan and tells us what we should be building, testing, and improving. You partner with our team on execution, but you lead the thinking on your work. - Deep comfort with Claude Code, GitHub, Supabase, Vercel, working with APIs, and the GoHighLevel API. We're also genuinely interested in whatever else you've built your workflow around. - A real love for this work. That comes through fast in conversation, and we'd rather hire someone who has it than someone who's just qualified. How to Apply A few things we'd love you to do, in order: 1. Please start your reply with the word "Jamal." We use this as a small check to spot applicants who read instructions carefully. Without it, we won't be able to review your application. 2. Record a 5-minute video answering the questions below. No script needed. We just want to get a feel for how you think. 3. Share your full tool stack with self-ratings (1 to 10). These ones we already know we need: - Claude Code /10 - GitHub /10 - Supabase /10 - Vercel /10 - Working with APIs /10 - GoHighLevel API /10 Then add every other tool you use, one per line, with /10. Include AI tools, frameworks, automation platforms, niche services, spreadsheets you've built, the works. Honesty matters more than scoring high. The video will give us a sense of where you actually are. 4. Walk us through your work history. For your last 2 or 3 clients or accounts: - What size was the company or account when you started (team size, what they were running, what was manual before you came in, whatever metric fits)? - What did you build for them? - What was the outcome? Where were they when you started, and where are they now? - If you have current clients, where are those projects today? We're trying to understand how you measure your own impact. The more specific, the better. 5. Quick confirmation that you can work somewhere inside the hours described above. How You Use AI In your video, spend a minute or so on this. The more specific the better: - Which AI tools you use daily, weekly, and occasionally - A specific example of a build task in this role where AI saves you real time, and how you use it - A time AI gave you a wrong or bad output. How you caught it, and what you did about it - Anything you've intentionally chosen NOT to let AI do in your work, and why We're looking for real, lived examples. The specifics tell the story. A Few Questions for Your Video Take these in order, and just talk through them naturally. 1. If you came on next week and we asked you to build out our internal tooling, walk us through your first 30 days. What would you want to look at, what would you build first, and how would you decide the order? 2. We hand you a vague request like "we need something to make X less painful," with no spec. Walk us through how you go from that sentence to a shipped tool. 3. An automation you built has started silently failing in production. Walk us through what you check first and how you track it down. 4. Why do you love building this kind of thing? Tell us about a recent tool or automation you shipped that reminded you why you do this work. 5. What's something about building with Claude Code, MCP, or agentic workflows that's changed in the last year that you've actually adjusted your approach around?

last_week_candidates.summary: HOW TO APPLY Fill out the Application form below: forms.gle/qgZgRCdD38XbhrEX6 Company: The FSW Group (ValveMan, FS Welsford, and our growing family of companies) Hours: Full-time | Remote, Philippines-based | 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM Eastern Time Compensation: $7 -...
last_week_candidates.full_description: HOW TO APPLY Fill out the Application form below: forms.gle/qgZgRCdD38XbhrEX6 Company: The FSW Group (ValveMan, FS Welsford, and our growing family of companies) Hours: Full-time | Remote, Philippines-based | 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM Eastern Time Compensation: $7 - $10 per hour (based on experience) WHO WE ARE We are a family business, and we mean that in every sense of the word. The FSW Group is a collection of companies, including ValveMan and Welsford Co., built over decades on relationships, trust, and a belief that how you treat people matters just as much as what you build. We are not a faceless corporation, and we are not looking to fill a seat. We are looking for someone to grow with us for years to come. When we say we treat our team like family, we are telling you what we expect of ourselves as much as what we offer you. We care about the people who build alongside us. We celebrate wins together, we work through challenges together, and we are genuinely invested in helping every team member build a career they are proud of. This is a long-term opportunity, not a gig. If you are someone who wants to keep sharpening your skills, take on bigger challenges over time, and build something meaningful with a company that has your back, we would love to meet you. WHAT WE OFFER A long-term home, not a short-term contract. We are looking for someone to build their career with us. A team that genuinely cares about you and treats you like family. The chance to work with cutting-edge AI tools every single day, and to keep growing your skills as the role expands. After six months of employment, as long as you are meeting all requirements of the role, you will receive five days of paid time off along with a stipend toward your HMO health insurance. Real ownership and real impact. Your work will directly improve how our companies run. OUR CORE VALUES These are not words on a wall. They are how we operate, and they are what we will look for in you. Integrity. Do what is right. Do what you say. Ambition. Continually level up. We want people who are always reaching for the next thing. Entrepreneurial Spirit. Take initiative, think like an owner, and find the better way. Supportive. Win together. Grow together. THE ROLE IN A NUTSHELL We are looking for a self-driven senior developer to become the engine behind how we use AI to improve our businesses. You will use Claude, Claude Code, Codex, and whatever is the best tool for the job at that time. You will own projects from the very first conversation all the way through to a working, deployed solution that people across our organization actually use each and every day. Post deployment, you will train team members on how to use the tool and continue to monitor the tool to assure it works. This is not a build-it-and-hand-it-off role. You will take an idea or an opportunity that may start out fuzzy, shape it into a clear plan, confirm it with the right people, build it, and then get it live and running. You are the owner from start to finish and beyond. The work is broad. Some projects will be classic software and web development. Others will be about streamlining how a part of the business runs, setting up automations and triggers, or building reporting and dashboards. Many will touch the Microsoft ecosystem, including Power Automate and Power BI, as well as Epicor Profit21 - both of which will be a meaningful part of this role. The common thread is that you are improving how the FSW Group operates, and you are using the best AI tools available to do it. WHAT YOU WILL ACTUALLY DO Take ownership of two to three projects at a time across the FSW Group, keeping your focus where it matters most without getting pulled in too many directions at once. Keep track of and manage the pipeline of project requests Understand the full picture of the projects on our plate, and gather complete, well-organized requirements for each one. Turn those requirements into a clear Statement of Work that everyone can agree on. Validate the requirements and the plan with the designated business leader, which depending on the project may be the leader of Welsford Co, the leader of ValveMan, or the CEO of the Group. Build the solution, using Claude, Claude Code, or the best tool/set of tools for that application. Deploy it. Getting something working as an MVP is only half the job. You are responsible for taking it all the way into a live, operational system that actually works in the real world. Work across whatever platforms the project requires, which may include our ecommerce systems, internal web applications, cloud infrastructure, automation tools, and especially the Epicor Profit21 and Microsoft platform through Power Automate and Power BI. Improve processes, not just code. Some of your work will be about rethinking how a workflow runs, adding the right triggers, and finding optimizations that touch any part of the organization. Keep an eye out for new opportunities. As you come to understand how things flow across our businesses, we want you to surface ideas for additional improvements we have not even thought to ask for yet. This is a secondary part of the role, but a valued one. WHO YOU ARE A genuinely strong developer with real experience architecting webapps as well as building and shipping software. Experienced with vibe coding (Claude Code, Codex, etc) specifically, and comfortable making AI tools the center of how you work. Someone with a real track record of architecting and deploying applications, not just prototyping them. You know the difference between “it runs on my machine” and “it is live and people depend on it.” You keep clear repos for handoffs and long term sustainability Capable of taking an ambiguous opportunity and running with it on your own. You can manage the whole pipeline, from requirements to delivery, without needing to be handed every step. Comfortable with the Microsoft platform, including Power Automate and Power BI, as well as Epicor Profit21 or confident you can get there quickly. A clear communicator who can talk to business leaders, understand what they actually need, and keep everyone aligned. Organized enough to juggle a few projects at once while keeping organized and focused on a quality high output. Excited to keep learning. This role will keep evolving, and we want someone who is eager to evolve with it. HOW WE WORK TOGETHER You will report to our Virtual Team Manager, who will help you prioritize, protect your focus, and make sure you have what you need to do great work. Your day-to-day business leader will vary by project, but you will always have a clear point of contact and clear direction on what success looks like. THE SCHEDULE This is a full-time role of 40 hours per week, working 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM Eastern Time (1 hour lunch break) so that you are fully aligned with our US team’s time. Forty hours is also the cap, because we believe in protecting your time and your energy. We are a family-first company, and that means we want you doing your best work during the day and then living your life when the day is done. THE TRIAL PROJECT Before we both fully commit, we will start with one real-world project together. It will be a small to medium sized project, the kind of work you would actually be doing in the role, and it will run from start to finish in roughly one week. You will own it the same way you would own anything else: gather the requirements, validate them, build it in Claude Code, and deploy it into a working system with proper documentation. This gives you a real feel for the work and our culture, and it gives us a chance to see your skills in action. This assures that the role/responsibilities will work for both parties moving forward.

last_week_candidates.full_description: ABOUT US: We're AdWave, a creative marketing agency based in Melbourne, Australia. We work with healthcare, cosmetic medicine, and lifestyle brands, handling everything from social media and content design to video editing and marketing strategy. We're a small, fast-moving team that leans heavily on AI tools (especially Claude / Claude Code) to work smarter, not harder. If you're someone who loves automating repetitive work, experimenting with AI workflows, and producing high-quality creative output, you'll fit right in. This is a full-time, long-term position. We're not looking for someone to fill a seat, we want a creative partner who grows with us. WHAT YOU'LL DO (a mix of everything): - Plan, schedule, and manage social media content (Instagram, LinkedIn, TikTok) across multiple client accounts - Create graphics and carousels (Canva / basic Figma) following brand guidelines - Edit short-form video (Reels, ads, promotional content) - Produce ad creatives for paid campaigns - Support e-commerce work (Shopify product content, basic store updates) - Build and maintain AI automation workflows (using Claude, Claude Code, and similar tools) - General virtual assistant tasks to keep projects moving - Create and manage SEO - Design and implement Website (Wordpress + Elementor). WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR: - Strong written English (most client-facing work is in English) - Experience with social media management and content creation - Solid graphic design skills (Canva essential, Figma and Photoshop.) - Video editing ability (CapCut, Premiere, or similar) - Genuine interest in AI tools — bonus points if you've used Claude or Claude Code - Self-starter who can work independently and communicate proactively - Reliable internet and a good working setup Nice to have (not required): - Experience with healthcare / beauty / cosmetic brands - Familiarity with Shopify - Knowledge of ad platforms (Meta Ads, Google Ads, Google console) WHY YOU'LL LIKE WORKING WITH US: - Work with cutting-edge AI tools every day, we genuinely invest in working this way - Variety — no two days are the same, you'll touch design, video, social, and automation - Long-term stability with a team that values your growth - Friendly, low-ego environment - Time zone overlap is easy (Philippines and Melbourne are only 2 hours apart) HOURS & PAY: - Full-time (40 hours/week) - Salary: [USD $800–1500/month, depending on experience] - Paid monthly via [Wise / PayPal] HOW TO APPLY: We read every application carefully. To show us you've actually read this post, please start your message with the word "AUTOMATE" applications without it won't be considered. In your application, please include: 1. A short intro about yourself and why this role fits you 2. Links to your portfolio (designs, videos, or social accounts you've managed) 3. Which AI tools you've used and how 4. Your expected monthly salary for full-time work Looking forward to meeting you!

We're Shoprev, a fast-growing marketing agency that helps automotive, home service, and local businesses generate more leads through marketing, automation, AI, and operational systems. We're looking for someone who genuinely loves building systems. last_week_candidates.full_description: Coordinate 3-5 contractors (scaling to 10-15) building AI-powered business systems across multiple concurrent projects. You'll translate technical specs into executed code, manage dependencies, and maintain quality standards. Remote, full-time (40 hours/week), Philippines-based, EST overlap required (4-6 hours daily). **CRITICAL REQUIREMENT:** You must be comfortable using AI coding assistants (Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, Cody, GitHub Copilot, etc.) to build production features daily. **WHAT YOU'LL DO:** Contractor Coordination (40%): - Break complex specs into contractor assignments - Track deliverables across multiple concurrent projects - Prevent duplicate work and dependency conflicts - Conduct code reviews against specifications - Resolve blockers and coordinate between developers Hands-On Development (30%): - Build proof-of-concepts with AI coding tools - Debug integration issues between contractor deliverables - Create reference implementations - Implement critical path items when contractors are blocked System Visibility (20%): - Track component ownership across all projects - Maintain production-readiness dashboards - Identify dependency blockers before they cascade Documentation (10%): - Maintain decision logs - Document shortcuts taken for speed - Build pattern libraries from successful implementations **REQUIRED EXPERIENCE:** Technical: - 3+ years coding (Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, or similar) - Experience with AI coding assistants for daily work - Production deployment experience - Modern frameworks (React, Node.js, FastAPI, or similar) - APIs and microservices architecture Coordination: - Coordinated 2+ developers on projects (formal management not required) - Breaking requirements into executable tasks - Code review experience - Multi-component systems delivered on schedule - Async coordination across time zones Communication: - Excellent written English for async work - GitHub/GitLab workflows and PR reviews - Comfortable working independently with weekly check-ins - Willing to push back when specs are unclear or timelines unrealistic **DIRECT COMMUNICATION REQUIRED:** We need someone who will tell us when specs are incomplete, flag issues early, challenge decisions that create technical problems, and say "this won't work" instead of silently struggling. **WORKING WITH THE FOUNDER:** 1. Founder writes comprehensive component specs 2. You break specs into contractor assignments 3. Contractors execute using AI-assisted development 4. You conduct technical validation and code review 5. You provide acceptance recommendations 6. Founder makes business decisions based on your assessment You maintain the component control system, track dependencies, conduct code reviews, handle tactical execution. **THIS ROLE ISN'T FOR YOU IF:** - You're not comfortable learning and using AI coding tools daily - You need daily standups instead of async coordination - You prefer single-project focus over managing multiple systems - You avoid telling leadership when they're wrong **TO APPLY, INCLUDE:** 1. Contractor Coordination Example: Your most complex multi-developer project (system, how you prevented conflicts, what went wrong, timeline, outcome) 2. Spec Writing Sample: Write contractor-ready spec (1-2 pages) for "Build an SMS-based collections system that coordinates with a database to track tenant payment status and automatically sends reminder messages." Include acceptance criteria. 3. Scenario Responses: A) Three contractors building interdependent services. One goes silent for 3 days. What do you do? B) Contractor delivers working code that doesn't match architectural patterns. How do you handle it? C) Two contractors independently created similar database tables with different schemas. How do you resolve it? D) Founder asks for a timeline you know is impossible. What do you say and how? 4. Logistics: Your timezone, EST overlap availability, start date, current hourly rate expectations ($7-10 range) **WAGE/SALARY PER MONTH:** $7-10/hour USD ($1,120-1,600/month for 40 hours/week) based on experience and AI tool proficiency. Performance bonuses: $200-500/month for hitting delivery and quality targets. Annual raises tied to portfolio growth and team expansion.

While SEO knowledge is a bonus, we're primarily looking for a skilled developer who's excited about Claude Code and willing to learn SEO as you build solutions. About Makarios Marketing Makarios Marketing is a results-driven SEO and digital marketing agency specializing in local service businesses and ecommerce brands. We're at the forefront of leveraging AI automation to build tools, dashboards, and workflows that solve real SEO problems for our clients and streamline our internal operations. content analysis, competitive research, and performance tracking Write clean, maintainable code that other developers (and clients) can understand and build upon Optimize Claude Code applications for performance, user experience, and accuracy Test and iterate on applications to ensure they deliver real value and work as intended Document your builds clearly so clients and team members can understand what you've created and how to use it Secondary: SEO Knowledge Application Learn SEO fundamentals as you build from our team (local SEO, backlinks, on-page optimization, technical SEO, etc.) Understand the SEO problems you're solving so you can build better, more relevant solutions Collaborate with our Head of SEO and team to understand client needs and translate them into automation opportunities Suggest new tool/automation ideas based on SEO gaps you identify Support the team in understanding what's possible with Claude Code for SEO workflows Tertiary: Leadership & Project Ownership Lead buildouts from concept to delivery, managing your own timeline and deliverables Communicate progress clearly with the Makarios Marketing team Manage client expectations around timelines and capabilities Be the expert voice on what's possible with Claude Code and when we should use it Required Qualifications & Experience Claude Code & Development Solid programming foundation - portfolio showing Claude Code projects you've built Understanding of APIs and how to integrate AI into applications Strong problem-solving skills and ability to debug and iterate Experience with full-stack development OR strong specialization in either frontend or backend (you don't need to be expert in both, but you need to be solid in at least one) SEO Knowledge (Bonus, but valuable) Any explain technical concepts clearly to non-technical clients and team members Curious learner - excited to learn both Claude Code deeper and SEO as you go Problem-solver - when something doesn't work, you figure it out rather than giving up Time zone flexible - willing to overlap with US working hours when needed for collaboration What You'll Submit With Your Application We want to see what you can do. Please include: Claude Code Portfolio (2-3 projects minimum) Links to or descriptions of Claude Code projects you've built What problem each project solved How you built it (brief technical overview) Results or feedback you received The more projects, the better - we want to see breadth and depth GitHub or Code Repository (if available) Any public code repositories showcasing your work Well-commented, clean code examples SEO Experience (if you have it) Any SEO projects or tools you've worked on Experience with SEO tools, platforms, or workflows Understanding of local SEO, backlinks, or other SEO areas Cover Letter Info on You Tell us about your Claude Code journey and what excites you about it WHY you stand out from others Specific examples of problems you've solved with Claude Code Why you're interested in combining Claude Code with SEO Your experience working on full-time remote projects What We Provide Full-time remote work from anywhere in the Philippines (or your location) Bonuses in pay for quick builds, and saving time Paid courses to increase skills Collaboration with our Head of SEO and Founder on strategy and direction Exposure to real client problems that your solutions will solve Opportunity to be an expert in an emerging technology at the intersection of AI and SEO Potential for growth as Claude Code capabilities expand Flexibility to work on both internal tools and client deliverables Compensation & Terms Compensation: $800-$1,200/month (based on experience and portfolio quality) Employment Type: Full-Time Contractor Work Arrangement: Remote Schedule: Full-time availability with flexibility around US collaboration hours Why This Role? last_week_candidates.full_description: I’m hiring an Executive Assistant | Automations Manager for St. Pierre. St. Pierre helps $1M-$10M foundation and waterproofing contractors keep a 3-6 week backlog of high-ticket jobs. This is not a basic VA role. This role is for someone who can work directly with the founder and clients, understand messy business problems, identify the real gap, design the fix, build the workflow, test it, document it, and make sure it works in the real world. Your job is to help turn repeated business work into reliable systems. You will work across: - GoHighLevel - CRMs - APIs - Webhooks - n8n, Make, Zapier, or similar tools - Google Sheets - Databases - Dashboards - Claude Code, Codex, and ChatGPT - Client onboarding, reporting, and troubleshooting workflows You do not need to be a traditional software developer. You do need strong English, clear writing, reliability, fast learning, and real experience using automation tools. The work will include: - Turning Loom videos, screenshots, transcripts, and notes into finished work - Turning repeated tasks into SOPs, checklists, workflows, and automations - Building or cleaning up GoHighLevel workflows - Building or improving Google Sheets trackers - Helping design dashboards and reporting systems - Drafting client updates when something needs explaining - Reviewing applicants and organizing recruiting pipelines - Researching tools, vendors, or competitors and recommending the best option - Helping with inbox, calendar, admin, reminders, and follow-ups - Supporting CRM, automation, onboarding, and troubleshooting projects You will work in 4-hour blocks. Each block should produce a clear finished output, such as: - a workflow - an SOP - a checklist - a tracker - a client update - a research memo - a dashboard spec - a fixed automation - a clear next-step plan You are a fit if: - You write clearly in English - You are organized and reliable - You can figure things out without needing every tiny step explained - You use Claude Code, Codex, ChatGPT, or similar tools to work faster - You have used tools like GoHighLevel, Google Sheets, Zapier, Make, or n8n - You can explain technical issues in plain English - You check your work before sending it back - You ask smart questions when needed - You want long-term work and more responsibility over time You are not a fit if: - You disappear when stuck - You need constant hand-holding - You send raw ChatGPT output without checking it - You say “done” when work is incomplete - You only want repetitive data entry - You cannot write clearly - You avoid messy problems - You build things without testing them How to apply: Do not send a generic message. Reply with: 1. A short intro 2. Your current availability 3. Your expected monthly pay for part-time work 4. Which tools you have used: Claude Code, Codex, ChatGPT, Google Sheets, GoHighLevel, Zapier, Make, n8n, Loom, Canva, etc. 5. Your strongest proof link: workflow, dashboard, SOP, tracker, automation, case study, Loom, or portfolio 6. A short Loom video showing: - your spoken English - your current Claude Code / Codex / ChatGPT workflow - one automation, workflow, tracker, or system you built - one messy problem you solved - one thing that broke and how you fixed it The best person can grow into a lead ops / automations manager role as we build the company.

last_week_candidates.full_description: **Job Title:** CAD / Takeoff / Metal Fabrication / Technical Assistant — Long-Term Remote Position **Company:** TCB Metal Works **Location:** Remote — Philippines **Type:** Part-Time to Full-Time **Hours:** 20–40 hours per week **Base Pay:** $5 USD per hour, with opportunities for increased pay and performance bonuses on profitable jobs We are a metal fabrication, welding, and machining shop in the United States looking for a highly capable, multi-skilled individual from the Philippines to join our team long-term. This is not a simple data-entry job. We are looking for an all-around problem solver who can help with CAD work, material takeoffs, shop drawings, estimating support, fabrication planning, technical research, product development, software projects, and sales/marketing systems. The right person should have experience or familiarity with structural steel, metal fabrication, welding, machining, and how metal parts are actually made in the real world. You do not need to know everything on day one, but you must be willing to learn quickly, figure things out, and take ownership of tasks even when they are unfamiliar. During busy times, the work may focus heavily on project takeoffs, CAD, shop drawings, estimating, and fabrication planning. During slower times, you may be helping engineer new products, design parts, build internal software using AI tools, create sales funnels, research new business ideas, improve systems, or help develop new revenue streams for the company. We need someone highly versatile. The ideal person is not limited to one narrow job description. We are looking for an all-star player who can move between technical, creative, research, software, and business tasks as needed. **Pay and Hours:** This position is for approximately **20–40 hours per week**, depending on workload, skill level, and performance. The base pay is **$5 USD per hour**. This base rate applies during slower periods, training periods, research tasks, internal projects, and general busy work. When we are working on highly profitable jobs, large fabrication projects, successful estimates, product development, or revenue-generating work, we are happy to increase pay and offer strong performance bonuses. We want someone who can grow with the company and be rewarded when they help create real value. For the right person, this can become a long-term role with increasing responsibility, higher pay opportunities, and bonuses based on performance and profitable work. **Main Responsibilities:** * Review architectural drawings, structural drawings, shop drawings, and PDFs * Perform material takeoffs for metal fabrication projects * Identify steel members, panels, plates, tubes, brackets, cages, railings, frames, and other fabricated components * Create or assist with CAD drawings and shop drawings * Help organize parts, assign part numbers, and break large projects into clear fabrication scopes * Assist with estimating by identifying materials, quantities, processes, and fabrication steps * Research materials, hardware, fabrication methods, welding requirements, and project details * programming when needed * Help build sales funnels, landing pages, lead lists, and marketing systems * Research new business opportunities and ways to improve company operations * Communicate clearly last_week_candidates.full_description: VersAssist is a fast-growing AI-powered execution company that helps entrepreneurs, authors, educators, and businesses bring ideas to life through content, automation, software, and digital products. We are looking for an AI-assisted Game Developer to join our growing game development department. You will work directly with our team to build educational, promotional, and commercial games using modern development tools and AI-assisted workflows. This is an excellent opportunity for a developer who enjoys creating games, learning new technologies, and leveraging AI tools to accelerate development. Our team actively uses tools such as Claude Code, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other AI platforms to improve productivity and speed up development cycles. RESPONSIBILITIES Develop and improve game projects for web, mobile, and desktop platforms. Work on gameplay systems, UI, menus, player progression, and game mechanics. Collaborate with project leads and other developers to implement new features. Use AI tools such as Claude Code and ChatGPT to assist with development, debugging, and documentation. Review and improve existing codebases. Fix bugs and optimize game performance. Create and maintain technical documentation. Participate in project planning and provide development estimates. Test builds and ensure game quality before release. Learn and adopt new AI-driven development workflows as they are introduced. Strong attention to detail. Good written English communication skills. Comfortable using AI tools as part of the development process. Reliable internet connection and computer. Preferred Experience publishing games to web, mobile, or desktop platforms. Experience with multiplayer systems. Experience with educational or simulation games. Familiarity with Git/GitHub. Experience working with remote teams. Understanding of AI-assisted coding workflows. COMPENSATION Starting Pay: $3.50 per hour for the first 100 hours. Incremental Increase: $0.50 increase per 100 hours worked until reaching $5.00 per hour. Expected Weekly Hours: 10+ hours per week, with potential for additional hours based on project workload and performance. BENEFITS Long-term opportunity with a growing company. Flexible working hours. Hands-on experience with cutting-edge AI development tools. Opportunity to work on multiple game projects. Career growth into senior developer or project leadership roles. Collaborative and supportive team environment. Access to AI-assisted development workflows and training. APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS To apply, please submit: Your resume. A brief introduction about your game development experience. Links to any games, GitHub repositories, demos, or portfolio projects. Your preferred game engine (Unity, Godot, Unreal, etc.). A short paragraph explaining how you currently use AI tools in your development workflow. IMPORTANT: Include the phrase "AI Game Builder" in the first line of your application so we know you read the entire job posting. We are looking for developers who are excited about the future of AI-assisted game development and want to grow alongside a rapidly expanding team.

last_week_candidates.summary: Local SEO Specialist (MUST HAVE AI AUTOMATION SKILLS IN LOCAL SEO) Full-Time - Makarios Marketing | [Remote] About the Role We're not looking for a traditional SEO hire. We're looking for a rare hybrid: someone who lives and breathes local SEO and thinks like...
last_week_candidates.full_description: Local SEO Specialist (MUST HAVE AI AUTOMATION SKILLS IN LOCAL SEO) Full-Time - Makarios Marketing | [Remote] About the Role We're not looking for a traditional SEO hire. We're looking for a rare hybrid: someone who lives and breathes local SEO and thinks like an AI engineer. You'll be responsible for delivering measurable search results for our clients while simultaneously building the tools, agents, and automations that put us years ahead of the competition. If you've ever thought, "I could automate this with an agent," while doing SEO work — this role was written for you. Read this entire post before applying. We mean it. Most SEO job posts get flooded with copy-paste applications from people who skimmed the title. This isn't that kind of job, and we're not that kind of company. If you're looking for a basic SEO role where you build a few links and write some meta descriptions, please move on. This role will frustrate you. If you're someone who loses sleep thinking about why a map pack result is where it is, and you've ever opened n8n or Claude Code just because you were curious — keep reading. Who We Are We're a results-driven SEO company that doesn't just execute strategies — we build the tools and systems that make those strategies unstoppable. We work in local SEO and we take it seriously. We're growing and we need someone who can grow with us, contribute ideas from day one, and help us deliver things to clients that no one else in the market is offering. What This Role Actually Is You will own local SEO strategy and execution across client accounts and you will build AI-powered tools and automations that make our work faster, smarter, and more valuable. These two responsibilities are equally important. You can't do one without the other here. This is not a junior role. This is not a "we'll train you on AI" role. You need to come in already capable. Your Day-to-Day Responsibilities Build and execute deep local SEO strategies — Google Business Profile, map pack optimization, local citations, geo-targeted content, and local link building Identify automation opportunities in our SEO workflows and build agents using Claude Code and n8n to handle them Create custom client-facing tools and reporting dashboards that go beyond anything a standard SEO platform offers Manage technical SEO audits, on-page optimization, and content strategy across multiple client sites Design and run link building campaigns that prioritize local authority and genuine relevance Monitor algorithm changes and translate them into concrete strategy shifts before clients even notice an impact Bring new ideas to the table — if you see a smarter way to do something, we want to hear it You Are Exactly Who We Need If: You have deep, real-world local SEO experience — you know how the local algorithm works, you've ranked businesses in competitive markets, and you can explain why something works, not just that it does You've built workflows or automations in n8n — not watched a tutorial, actually built something You've used Claude Code to build or deploy AI agents in a real context You've worked with OpenClaw or similar AI platforms to extend what's possible You can write enough code (Python, JavaScript, or similar) to build tools that actually work You've managed SEO across multiple client accounts simultaneously You think in systems — you see every repetitive task as a future automation You communicate clearly in written English with clients and teammates You Are Not Who We Need If: Your experience is mostly on-page basics and you've never touched automation You've "heard of" n8n or Claude Code but never opened them You apply to every job posting with the same template message You need hand-holding to figure out what to prioritize You're looking for a role you can coast in You've done some SEO, but never specialized in just that We will not respond to generic applications. If your opening message doesn't show us you read this post, it goes straight to the archive. Tell us why you stand out, what you bring, how you will stand out from others. We get tons of applications. STAND OUT Include code SEO 26 somewhere in your application — this tells us you read everything and follow instructions, two things that are non-negotiable in this role What We Offer Full-time, long-term position — we're not looking for a short contract, we're looking for someone to grow with us Bonuses for performance as well as paid trainings and courses you need or want Competitive monthly salary based on your experience and skills Full access to the tools you need — AI platforms, SEO software, automation tools Real creative freedom — if you have an idea for a tool or system, you can build it A team that actually values what you bring and won't micromanage how you do it How to Apply Send your application and include all of the following or it will not be considered: A specific example of a local SEO win you're proud of — what was the situation, what did you do, what happened One automation or AI tool you've built — describe what it does and what you used to build it Share documents, agents, work you've done that is relevant Applications without all three of the above will not receive a response. No exceptions. This role is for the person who sees a gap in what's possible and immediately starts thinking about how to close it. If that's you, we'd love to hear from you. Monthly - $800-1200 USD for 40 hours a week (negotiable on experience) Must be willing to work 9am - 5pm EST

This is an **ongoing role**, not a one-time project. --- ## **What You’ll Be Responsible For** • Completing unfinished pages and flows across multiple platforms • Fixing “Failed to fetch” and data rendering issues • Finalizing CRM systems and linked records • Completing Product pages and CRUD functionality • Building and refining advanced forms and modals • Ensuring data consistency across desktop and mobile • Polishing UI/UX so no page feels incomplete • Maintaining platform stability as new features are added You will actively help **fully complete**: GasMask (primary focus to start) Unforgettable Times TopTier Experience Playboxxx The Core OS (Global CRM, Brand CRMs, Admin tools) ## **Required Skills (Non-Negotiable)** • Strong hands-on experience with **Lovable.dev** • Working knowledge of **Supabase** (tables, relationships, CRUD, RLS) • Ability to debug and fix data fetching issues • Experience working on **CRM or business platforms** • Comfortable refining existing systems (not just starting new ones) • Attention to detail and ability to follow structured task lists • Ability to work independently and communicate clearly You can paste this directly: Required Skills • Hands-on experience with Lovable.dev • Working knowledge of Supabase (tables, relationships, CRUD) • Ability to debug and fix data rendering issues • Experience completing and polishing existing platforms • CRM or business platform experience • Strong bug-fixing and attention to detail • Responsive across desktop and mobile • Clear WhatsApp communication and daily availability If you cannot troubleshoot why something works on mobile but not desktop, this role is not a fit. --- ## **UI / Brand Awareness** You must respect brand color systems and platform identity, including: . last_week_candidates.full_description: **About the role** I run a fast-growing AI consulting business serving high-profile entrepreneurs in the German-speaking market. We help them work with Claude (Cowork) and we build the setups and skills that run their business. We already run client Claude setups, email systems on client servers, dashboards, and sales workflows live. I have more client projects and more ideas than the team can execute, so I'm bringing on one more builder. This is not heavy coding. It is knowing how to use Claude and Claude Code the right way: building skills cleanly, testing properly, and shipping things that actually work. **Who you'll work with** You won't be alone. You'll join a small, tight team and work directly with me and another developer. Clear structures, clear briefings, daily contact. Lots of guidance early, more freedom as trust grows. **What you will do** - Build and test Claude skills (for clients using Claude Cowork and for internal use) - Set up and run client systems on their VPS servers (email: classify and structure mail, auto-draft replies, pre-filtering, black- and whitelists by sender and topic) - Build dashboards and controlling from Excel exports (turning platform exports into clean revenue and commission numbers) - Content and design skills, reusable templates (keynotes, presentations) - Proposal and invoice workflows - Lead scraping and enrichment, end-to-end sales workflows - Build custom MCPs that connect a client's Claude Cowork setup to their VPS - Turn finished projects into reusable templates we deploy across many clients Most client projects are not advanced. The work is simple but takes time, and it has to be done carefully and correctly. **What matters most to me** Two things above everything: honesty and reliability. When you tell me a skill or a system is done and working, I need to fully trust that. I do not want to re-check your work. "Done" has to mean done and tested. My clients are in the German-speaking market, so data protection (GDPR) and security are always at the highest level. Precise, careful, conscientious work is the foundation. **Must-have** - Hands-on experience with Claude / Claude Code (Cowork): able to build, test and ship skills - VPS / server skills: comfortable setting up and running systems on a Linux server - Strategic thinking: if I send you a discovery-call transcript, you can suggest how you would build it and why - Follows instructions precisely and works in a structured way - Honest and reliable - Detail-oriented and careful (data protection and security) - Good written English **What I offer** - Long-term, full-time collaboration, not short project hopping - A growing company with high-profile clients and a real team around you - Clear structures, clear organization, very clear instructions - A performance culture: I do not care how long you take or how you got there, I care about the result. Strong, fast, high-quality work is rewarded with raises, quickly **Hours** We work on Gulf Standard Time (Dubai, GMT+4). Team hours are 10 AM to 6 PM Dubai time, which is 2 PM to 10 PM your time in the Philippines. We have a short daily check-in call, and I want a short written end-of-day report every day. **Compensation** Starting salary $800 USD/month. We start lean. Performance is rewarded fast: above-average work means steady raises and growing income over time. The person we hired last round is already on this path. **Who should not apply** Please don't apply if you're a general virtual assistant. We need a specialized Claude expert, not a VA. If your background is general IT support or make.com / n8n automation, this is not the right fit either. The projects you've built have to be built with Claude. We do NOT use n8n or make.com or zapier. **How to apply** Don't send me a portfolio or a long write-up. Just reply with one line: "I'm in". That's it. I answer everyone, and we take it from there together. Applications close **Tuesday, June 30, EOD**.

last_week_candidates.full_description: We're looking for a sharp, tech-curious right hand who loves using AI to make things run faster and smarter. The heart of this role is using AI to build — simple automations, internal tools, and reports that save our small team hours every week. If you get a little thrill from figuring out a new tool, automating something tedious, and making a process better than you found it, you'll fit right in. It's a mixed role, and we want to be upfront about that. The AI, automation, and efficiency side is the core of what we're hiring for — but you'll also help with customer service for our clean Christian music app, a bit of marketing, and general admin, and you need to be genuinely open to pitching in wherever the team needs you. We use tools like Claude (and Claude Code) every day. You don't need to have done this before — but you do need to be excited to learn it. If you anticipate what's needed before being asked, take real ownership, communicate like a pro, and love learning something new — keep reading. We're hiring the person, not the résumé. Technical curiosity and drive matter most; the rest we can teach — including how we build with AI. What you'll do Build, automate, and make us more efficient with AI — use Claude / ChatGPT (and grow into Claude Code) to automate repetitive work, build simple internal tools, draft content, and find smarter, faster ways for the team to operate. This is the core of the role. We'll teach you; you bring the curiosity. Turn data into insight — pull together clear daily reports and spot patterns (with AI's help). Bring us insights, not just numbers. Be my right hand — anticipate needs, follow through without reminders, flag issues early, and close loops. Support our clean Christian music app — customer service, content, and launch-related help. Pitch in on the rest — influencer outreach, light e-commerce / Etsy support, and general admin as we grow. A real love of music is a big plus — you'll be working in it every day. Who you are (this matters most to us) Technically curious — this is the big one. You enjoy poking at new tools, you're energized (not intimidated) by AI, and when you see a repetitive task your first instinct is "can I automate this?" Any interest in coding, automation, scripting, or tinkering is a real plus. Great — not necessarily experienced. Eager, smart, proactive, and resourceful. We'd take a brilliant fast-learner over a seasoned coaster every single time. A self-starter who takes initiative instead of waiting to be told, and teaches themselves what they don't know. Open and flexible — happy to jump from an automation project to a customer-service question to a quick admin task without missing a beat. You're here for the technical work, but you don't consider anything "below" you. A clear communicator — responsive, easy to work with, and good at keeping us in the loop. Reliable and professional — you do what you say you'll do, and you have a stable internet connection good enough to hold a video call and screen-share without dropping. Honest and accountable — you log the time you actually work and speak up early when something's off. This one is non-negotiable. Detail-oriented. To show us you read carefully, please mention your favorite color in your application where it asks how you heard about us. Mission-aligned — you genuinely connect with what we're building: clean, faith-friendly music for families. Helpful (but NOT required — we'll train you) AI & automation — bonus points for hands-on Claude / Claude Code experience, automation work (n8n, Make, Zapier), or any interest in coding or scripting. Using ChatGPT, Claude, or other AI tools in your daily workflow. Data analysis (with AI's help) to generate reports and spot trends. Pinterest / social media scheduling tools. Customer service experience. WordPress publishing and AI image generation (e.g., Ideogram, gpt-image). Influencer outreach and management. The details: Full-time, 40 hrs/week, long-term. We start at $400–500/month and there's room to grow — in both pay and responsibility — as you take on more and grow into the AI/automation side. Based in the Philippines, with some flexibility to occasionally overlap U.S. Mountain Time for a quick call or chat. We use the time tracker Trackabi for active work hours. Benefits Flexible hours — days, nights, spread out, or catch up on weekends. PTO after 6 months full-time. Paid training time to learn new skills on the clock. A genuinely great team and culture. I treat my team like friends, not just freelancers — many have been with me for years, with no plans of leaving. How to apply Apply through the Google Form here: forms.gle/6P8ZySUcbKz67U5U6— that's the only way to apply, so please don't DM or email instead. Two quick things in the form: When it asks about your experience with AI tools, tell us about a time you taught yourself a new tool, or automated or figured out something you'd never done before — what you did and how it turned out. There's no "right" answer; we just want to see how you think and learn. We're hiring one person and reviewing applications as they come in. Top candidates get a paid test project — your real chance to show us how you work. If the AI and automation side sounds more like a chore than a challenge you'd enjoy, this probably isn't the right fit — and that's completely okay. But if everything above sounds like you, we'd genuinely love to meet you.

SMASH is not a simple brochure site.
