We need a developer who can step into an existing production system, understand how the business works, improve what is already there, and build new features that help our sales, marketing, operations, and leadership teams move faster. We use Timeproof and Easy Pay. What You’ll Work On You’ll help maintain, improve, and build features across SMASH and related internal tools, including: • Lead management and sales pipeline workflows • Order and booking flows • Reporting dashboards and operational reports • Vendor, ticket, voucher, and reconciliation tools • CRM automation and follow-up systems • Integrations with external APIs and internal services • Performance, reliability, and data-quality improvements • UX improvements for internal teams who use the system every day We are also in a large AI push to implement more AI technology and automation workflows to increase overall efficiency. Core Technology Stack The ideal candidate should be comfortable with: • C# / .NET • _ASP.NET Core_ • Entity Framework / EF migrations • SQL Server • Stored procedures, views, reporting queries, and data troubleshooting • Angular / TypeScript • HTML, CSS, responsive UI work • REST APIs and service-layer architecture • Git-based source control • Azure DevOps, pull requests, branches, and work items Experience with any of these is a plus: • ASP.NET Boilerplate / ASP.NET Zero • PrimeNG or similar Angular UI frameworks • Background jobs, scheduled jobs, or automation workflows • CRM, booking, ticketing, reservation, or ecommerce systems • Reporting systems and business analytics • API integrations • AI-assisted development tools such as Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, etc. What Makes Someone a Great Fit We are looking for someone who is more than just a ticket-taker. The right developer should be able to: • Read and understand an existing codebase without needing everything rewritten • Diagnose bugs from real user reports and production data • Write clean, maintainable code that fits the current system • Work confidently with SQL Server and business-critical data • Understand business workflows before changing them • Communicate clearly with non-technical team members • Think through edge cases, reporting accuracy, and operational impact • Own a problem from investigation through deployment • Balance speed with quality Examples of Projects You Might Handle • Build a new inbox/view for a specific lead or order type • Add filters, columns, and export options to an internal report • Fix a bug in how orders, hooks, or statuses are counted • Improve a sales agent workflow to reduce manual work • Add an integration between SMASH and another internal/external system • Update Angular screens for better usability • Create or modify SQL reports used by management • Improve data consistency between leads, contacts, orders, and campaigns Required Experience • 3+ years professional software development experience • Strong C#/.NET experience • Strong SQL Server experience • Experience building or maintaining web applications • Ability to work in an existing production codebase • Comfortable debugging across frontend, backend, and database layers Preferred Experience • Angular experience • CRM, booking, travel, ticketing, or sales operations software experience • Azure DevOps experience • Experience with reporting-heavy business systems • Experience modernizing older systems without breaking daily operations What We Value We value developers who are practical, reliable, and curious. SMASH supports real people doing real work every day, so the best fit is someone who cares about usability, accuracy, and business impact. You do not need to be flashy. You do need to be dependable, thoughtful, and capable of shipping useful improvements.

last_week_candidates.full_description: About the Role We're looking for a developer who lives at the intersection of AI and automation. You'll design and build fully autonomous creative pipelines that take a single prompt and produce complete, polished advertisements — visuals, video, copy, and editing — with no manual intervention and other sorts of Ai agents that helps our beauty brand Faccelove function more efficiently. You'll work with Claude Code as the orchestration backbone, integrating third-party generative AI services (NanoBanana for image generation, Higgsfield for AI video, and others) into seamless, production-grade workflows. The end goal: a system where a human provides a brief and gets back a finished creative asset. What You'll Build - Autonomous AI agent pipelines using Claude Code that chain together multiple generative AI services end-to-end - API integrations with image generation (NanoBanana), video generation (Higgsfield), and editing/post-production tools - Prompt engineering systems that translate high-level creative briefs into structured, multi-step agent workflows - Quality control and feedback loops — automated checks that evaluate outputs and retry/refine when needed - Robust error handling, state management, and logging for complex multi-API orchestration Requirements (Must-Have) - Hands-on experience building AI agents or agentic workflows (Claude Code, LangChain, CrewAI, or similar frameworks) - Strong proficiency in Python and/or TypeScript — you write clean, production-grade code, not just scripts - Experience with LLM APIs: function calling / tool use, structured outputs, prompt chaining, and multi-turn orchestration - Proven ability to integrate and orchestrate multiple third-party APIs in a single automated pipeline - Solid understanding of async programming, error handling, retry logic, and building resilient systems Nice-to-Have - Familiarity with generative media APIs — image generation, video generation, or AI-driven editing tools - Experience with creative production workflows (advertising, content creation, or media post-production) - Background in MCP (Model Context Protocol), tool servers, or building custom tool integrations for LLMs Tech Stack - Claude Code, Anthropic API, Python, TypeScript, NanoBanana, Higgsfield, MCP, REST APIs, Webhooks, Git What Sets You Apart You don't just use AI tools — you build systems that make AI tools work together autonomously. You've shipped at least one project where an AI agent completes a multi-step task end-to-end without human intervention. You understand that the hardest part isn't the individual API calls — it's the orchestration, the edge cases, and making the whole pipeline reliable at scale. You're comfortable in ambiguity. This space is new and moving fast. You'll often be the first person to figure out how to make two services talk to each other in a way nobody has documented yet.

last_week_candidates.summary: AI-Powered SEO Specialist Full-Time - Makarios Marketing] | [Remote] About the Role We're not looking for a traditional SEO hire. We're looking for a rare hybrid: someone who lives and breathes local SEO and thinks like an AI engineer. You'll be responsible...
last_week_candidates.full_description: AI-Powered SEO Specialist Full-Time - Makarios Marketing] | [Remote] About the Role We're not looking for a traditional SEO hire. We're looking for a rare hybrid: someone who lives and breathes local SEO and thinks like an AI engineer. You'll be responsible for delivering measurable search results for our clients while simultaneously building the tools, agents, and automations that put us years ahead of the competition. If you've ever thought "I could automate this with an agent" while doing SEO work — this role was written for you. Read this entire post before applying. We mean it. Most SEO job posts get flooded with copy-paste applications from people who skimmed the title. This isn't that kind of job, and we're not that kind of company. If you're looking for a basic SEO role where you build a few links and write some meta descriptions, please move on. This role will frustrate you. If you're someone who loses sleep thinking about why a map pack result is where it is, and you've ever opened n8n or Claude Code just because you were curious — keep reading. Who We Are We're a results-driven SEO company that doesn't just execute strategies — we build the tools and systems that make those strategies unstoppable. We work in local SEO and we take it seriously. We're growing and we need someone who can grow with us, contribute ideas from day one, and help us deliver things to clients that no one else in the market is offering. What This Role Actually Is You will own local SEO strategy and execution across client accounts and you will build AI-powered tools and automations that make our work faster, smarter, and more valuable. These two responsibilities are equally important. You can't do one without the other here. This is not a junior role. This is not a "we'll train you on AI" role. You need to come in already capable. Your Day-to-Day Responsibilities Build and execute deep local SEO strategies — Google Business Profile, map pack optimization, local citations, geo-targeted content, and local link building Identify automation opportunities in our SEO workflows and build agents using Claude Code and n8n to handle them Create custom client-facing tools and reporting dashboards that go beyond anything a standard SEO platform offers Manage technical SEO audits, on-page optimization, and content strategy across multiple client sites Design and run link building campaigns that prioritize local authority and genuine relevance Monitor algorithm changes and translate them into concrete strategy shifts before clients even notice an impact Bring new ideas to the table — if you see a smarter way to do something, we want to hear it You Are Exactly Who We Need If: You have deep, real-world local SEO experience — you know how the local algorithm works, you've ranked businesses in competitive markets, and you can explain why something works, not just that it does You've built workflows or automations in n8n — not watched a tutorial, actually built something You've used Claude Code to build or deploy AI agents in a real context You've worked with OpenClaw or similar AI platforms to extend what's possible You can write enough code (Python, JavaScript, or similar) to build tools that actually work You've managed SEO across multiple client accounts simultaneously You think in systems — you see every repetitive task as a future automation You communicate clearly in written English with clients and teammates You Are Not Who We Need If: Your experience is mostly on-page basics and you've never touched automation You've "heard of" n8n or Claude Code but never opened them You apply to every job posting with the same template message You need hand-holding to figure out what to prioritize You're looking for a role you can coast in We will not respond to generic applications. If your opening message doesn't show us you read this post, it goes straight to the archive. Include code SEO 26 somewhere in your application — this tells us you read everything and follow instructions, two things that are non-negotiable in this role What We Offer Full-time, long-term position — we're not looking for a short contract, we're looking for someone to grow with us Competitive monthly salary based on your experience and skills Full access to the tools you need — AI platforms, SEO software, automation tools Real creative freedom — if you have an idea for a tool or system, you can build it A team that actually values what you bring and won't micromanage how you do it Bonuses for performance as well as paid trainings and courses you need or want How to Apply Send your application and include all of the following or it will not be considered: A specific example of a local SEO win you're proud of — what was the situation, what did you do, what happened One automation or AI tool you've built — describe what it does and what you used to build it Share documents, agents, work you've done that is relevant Applications without all three of the above will not receive a response. No exceptions. This role is for the person who sees a gap in what's possible and immediately starts thinking about how to close it. If that's you, we'd love to hear from you. Monthly - $800-1200 USD for 40 hours a week (negotiable on experience) Must be willing to work 9am - 5pm EST

last_week_candidates.title: Right-Hand VA (AI & Tech Focus) — Clean Christian Music App
last_week_candidates.summary: We're looking for a sharp, tech-curious right hand who loves using AI to make things run faster and smarter. The heart of this role is using AI to build — simple automations, internal tools, and reports that save our small team hours every week. If you get a...
last_week_candidates.full_description: We're looking for a sharp, tech-curious right hand who loves using AI to make things run faster and smarter. The heart of this role is using AI to build — simple automations, internal tools, and reports that save our small team hours every week. If you get a little thrill from figuring out a new tool, automating something tedious, and making a process better than you found it, you'll fit right in. It's a mixed role, and we want to be upfront about that. The AI, automation, and efficiency side is the core of what we're hiring for — but you'll also help with customer service for our clean Christian music app, a bit of marketing, and general admin, and you need to be genuinely open to pitching in wherever the team needs you. We use tools like Claude (and Claude Code) every day. You don't need to have done this before — but you do need to be excited to learn it. If you anticipate what's needed before being asked, take real ownership, communicate like a pro, and love learning something new — keep reading. We're hiring the person, not the résumé. Technical curiosity and drive matter most; the rest we can teach — including how we build with AI. What you'll do Build, automate, and make us more efficient with AI — use Claude / ChatGPT (and grow into Claude Code) to automate repetitive work, build simple internal tools, draft content, and find smarter, faster ways for the team to operate. This is the core of the role. We'll teach you; you bring the curiosity. Turn data into insight — pull together clear daily reports and spot patterns (with AI's help). Bring us insights, not just numbers. Be my right hand — anticipate needs, follow through without reminders, flag issues early, and close loops. Support our clean Christian music app — customer service, content, and launch-related help. Pitch in on the rest — influencer outreach, light e-commerce / Etsy support, and general admin as we grow. A real love of music is a big plus — you'll be working in it every day. Who you are (this matters most to us) Technically curious — this is the big one. You enjoy poking at new tools, you're energized (not intimidated) by AI, and when you see a repetitive task your first instinct is "can I automate this?" Any interest in coding, automation, scripting, or tinkering is a real plus. Great — not necessarily experienced. Eager, smart, proactive, and resourceful. We'd take a brilliant fast-learner over a seasoned coaster every single time. A self-starter who takes initiative instead of waiting to be told, and teaches themselves what they don't know. Open and flexible — happy to jump from an automation project to a customer-service question to a quick admin task without missing a beat. You're here for the technical work, but you don't consider anything "below" you. A clear communicator — responsive, easy to work with, and good at keeping us in the loop. Reliable and professional — you do what you say you'll do, and you have a stable internet connection good enough to hold a video call and screen-share without dropping. Honest and accountable — you log the time you actually work and speak up early when something's off. This one is non-negotiable. Detail-oriented. To show us you read carefully, please mention your favorite color in your application where it asks how you heard about us. Mission-aligned — you genuinely connect with what we're building: clean, faith-friendly music for families. Helpful (but NOT required — we'll train you) AI & automation — bonus points for hands-on Claude / Claude Code experience, automation work (n8n, Make, Zapier), or any interest in coding or scripting. Using ChatGPT, Claude, or other AI tools in your daily workflow. Data analysis (with AI's help) to generate reports and spot trends. Pinterest / social media scheduling tools. Customer service experience. WordPress publishing and AI image generation (e.g., Ideogram, gpt-image). Influencer outreach and management. The details: Full-time, 40 hrs/week, long-term. We start at $400–500/month and there's room to grow — in both pay and responsibility — as you take on more and grow into the AI/automation side. Based in the Philippines, with some flexibility to occasionally overlap U.S. Mountain Time for a quick call or chat. We use the time tracker Trackabi for active work hours. Benefits Flexible hours — days, nights, spread out, or catch up on weekends. PTO after 6 months full-time. Paid training time to learn new skills on the clock. A genuinely great team and culture. I treat my team like friends, not just freelancers — many have been with me for years, with no plans of leaving. How to apply Apply through the Google Form here: forms.gle/6P8ZySUcbKz67U5U6— that's the only way to apply, so please don't DM or email instead. Two quick things in the form: When it asks about your experience with AI tools, tell us about a time you taught yourself a new tool, or automated or figured out something you'd never done before — what you did and how it turned out. There's no "right" answer; we just want to see how you think and learn. We're hiring one person and reviewing applications as they come in. Top candidates get a paid test project — your real chance to show us how you work. If the AI and automation side sounds more like a chore than a challenge you'd enjoy, this probably isn't the right fit — and that's completely okay. But if everything above sounds like you, we'd genuinely love to meet you.

last_week_candidates.summary: AI & Automation Specialist (Full-Time Remote) Salary $600-$1,000 USD per month (depending on experience) Location Remote (Philippines Preferred) About Us We are a fast-growing eCommerce business operating across Amazon, TikTok Shop, wholesale, private label...
last_week_candidates.full_description: AI & Automation Specialist (Full-Time Remote) Salary $600-$1,000 USD per month (depending on experience) Location Remote (Philippines Preferred) About Us We are a fast-growing eCommerce business operating across Amazon, TikTok Shop, wholesale, private label, and international marketplaces. We're looking for a full-time AI & Automation Specialist whose primary responsibility is simple: Make the business more efficient every single week. You will work directly with the founder, identify repetitive tasks, build automations, improve workflows, and leverage AI tools to save time across the company. Your Mission Your goal is to help the founder and team achieve more in less time. You should constantly be asking: What can be automated? What can be delegated to AI? What tasks are wasting time? How can we improve existing workflows? What processes can be simplified? You will be expected to actively suggest improvements rather than simply waiting for instructions. Responsibilities AI & Automation Build workflows using AI tools Create automations using platforms such as Make, N8N, Zapier and similar tools Connect software using APIs where possible Research and test new AI tools AI Assistants Create custom GPTs and AI assistants Improve prompts and workflows Build internal knowledge bases Create AI systems for customer service, operations, and administration Business Operations Review existing processes Document SOPs Identify inefficiencies Suggest improvements Help implement new systems Research & Development Stay updated on the latest AI developments Test new tools and software Present ideas that could save time or money Required Skills Essential Strong experience using Claude Strong experience using ChatGPT Experience with AI prompting Excellent English communication Strong analytical skills Ability to learn quickly Attention to detail Preferred Make.com N8N Zapier Airtable Notion Google Workspace Python API integrations E-commerce experience What We're Looking For We want someone who: ? Loves AI ? Is constantly learning new tools ? Thinks independently ? Enjoys solving problems ? Looks for ways to improve processes ? Can communicate ideas clearly ? Is excited about building systems KPIs Success in this role will be measured by: Number of automations implemented Hours saved per month Process improvements delivered Quality of ideas suggested Speed of implementation Overall impact on business efficiency To Apply Please send: CV/Resume A short Loom video (3-5 minutes) Examples of AI workflows or automations you have built List of AI tools you use regularly Your internet speed Your expected salary Bonus Question If you joined our company tomorrow, what are the first 3 areas of a growing eCommerce business you would try to automate and why?

last_week_candidates.skill_requirement: Email Marketing Influencer Marketing Social Media Marketing
last_week_candidates.summary: We're looking for a sharp, creative, trend-obsessed marketer who lives on social media and loves using AI to make great content faster and smarter. The heart of this role is organic social — finding what's working right now, testing new content types before...
last_week_candidates.full_description: We're looking for a sharp, creative, trend-obsessed marketer who lives on social media and loves using AI to make great content faster and smarter. The heart of this role is organic social — finding what's working right now, testing new content types before everyone else does, and turning scrolls into follows, clicks, and customers. You'll have real ownership over what we post, how it performs, and where we grow next. If you get a little thrill from spotting a trend early, trying a format nobody's done yet, watching the analytics climb, and making a piece of content better than you found it — you'll fit right in. You don't need to have done all of this before, but you do need to be excited to learn. We use AI every day, and you'll use it to move faster: drafting, repurposing, researching, and creating gorgeous visuals. It's a mixed role, and we want to be upfront about that. Organic social and marketing are the core of what we're hiring for — but you'll also help with customer service for our clean Christian music app, a bit of general admin, and whatever else a small, growing team needs. You need to be genuinely open to pitching in wherever we need you. We use tools like Claude (and Claude Code) every day, and we'll teach you how we work. If you anticipate what's needed before being asked, take real ownership, communicate like a pro, and love learning something new — keep reading. We're hiring the person, not the résumé. Creativity, instincts, and drive matter most; the rest we can teach — including the AI tools we build with. What you'll do Own our organic social (the core). Plan, create, and post across our platforms; catch trends early and act on them fast; test new content types and formats; build engaged audiences and keep them growing. This is the heart of the role. Make scroll-stopping content — with an eye for design. Create visuals that look premium and on-brand, using AI image tools (Midjourney, Ideogram, gpt-image, and friends) to produce gorgeous images quickly. Good taste matters here. Grow beyond organic. Pitch in on email marketing, funnels, paid ads, and influencer marketing as we scale. You don't need to be an expert in all of it — but you should be eager to learn it and run with it. Turn data into insight. Monitor analytics across platforms, spot what's working (and what's not), and bring us clear takeaways — not just numbers. Then double down on the winners. Use AI to work faster and smarter. Lean on Claude / ChatGPT (and grow into Claude Code) to draft content, repurpose it across platforms, research, and automate the tedious parts of content ops. We'll teach you; you bring the curiosity. Be my right hand. Anticipate needs, follow through without reminders, flag issues early, and close loops. Support our clean Christian music app — content, community, customer service, and launch-related help. Pitch in on the rest — light e-commerce / Etsy support and general admin as we grow. A real love of music is a big plus — you'll be working in it every day. Who you are (this matters most to us) Creative and trend-obsessed — this is the big one. You live on social, you catch trends before they peak, and you have strong instincts for what makes people stop, save, and share. An eye for design. You know what looks good, and you can create (or AI-assist) visuals that feel premium and on-brand. Very proactive — a true self-starter. You take initiative instead of waiting to be told, teach yourself what you don't know, and your first instinct on a repetitive task is "can I make this faster?" Tech-curious and AI-friendly. You're energized (not intimidated) by AI tools, and you're excited to use them every day to create and move faster. Any interest in automation or tinkering is a real plus. Comfortable with numbers. You actually look at the analytics and let them guide your next test. Great — not necessarily experienced. Eager, smart, proactive, and resourceful. We'd take a brilliant fast-learner over a seasoned coaster every single time. Open and flexible. Happy to jump from a content project to a customer-service question to a quick admin task without missing a beat. You're here for the creative and marketing work, but you don't consider anything "below" you. A clear communicator — responsive, easy to work with, and good at keeping us in the loop. Reliable and professional — you do what you say you'll do, and you have a stable internet connection good enough to hold a video call and screen-share without dropping. Honest and accountable — you log the time you actually work and speak up early when something's off. This one is non-negotiable. Detail-oriented. To show us you read carefully, please mention your favorite color in your application where it asks how you heard about us. Mission-aligned — you genuinely connect with what we're building: clean, faith-friendly music for families. Helpful (but NOT required — we'll train you) Hands-on organic social experience (TikTok, Instagram / Reels, Pinterest, YouTube Shorts, Facebook). Any experience with email marketing, funnels, paid ads (Meta, etc.), or influencer marketing. AI image generation (Midjourney, Ideogram, gpt-image) and design tools (Canva, Figma, etc.). Using ChatGPT, Claude, or other AI tools in your daily workflow — bonus points for Claude / Claude Code or automation (n8n, Make, Zapier). Reading analytics to spot trends and report results. Pinterest / social media scheduling tools. Customer service experience. WordPress publishing. Influencer outreach and management. The details Full-time, 40 hrs/week, long-term. We start at $400–500/month and there's room to grow — in both pay and responsibility — as you take on more and grow into the wider marketing and AI side. Based in the Philippines, with some flexibility to occasionally overlap U.S. Mountain Time for a quick call or chat. We use the time tracker Trackabi for active work hours. Benefits Flexible hours — days, nights, spread out, or catch up on weekends. PTO after 6 months full-time. Paid training time to learn new skills on the clock. A genuinely great team and culture. I treat my team like friends, not just freelancers — many have been with me for years, with no plans of leaving. How to apply Apply through the Google Form here: forms.gle/FYMdZ3h6LzWd1H5GA — that's the only way to apply, so please don't DM or email instead. Two quick things in the form: When it asks about your experience with AI tools, tell us about a time you taught yourself something new — a tool, a content format, or a campaign — what you did and how it turned out. There's no "right" answer; we just want to see how you think and learn. We're hiring one person and reviewing applications as they come in. Top candidates get a paid test project — your real chance to show us how you work. If creating content, chasing trends, and using AI to do it all faster sounds more like a chore than something you'd genuinely enjoy, this probably isn't the right fit — and that's completely okay. But if everything above sounds like you, we'd genuinely love to meet you.

last_week_candidates.title: AI Automation & Workflow Development Specialist
last_week_candidates.skill_requirement: API Development Software Development Project Manager AI Automation
last_week_candidates.summary: About the Role We are looking for an AI Automation & Workflow Development Specialist to design, build, and maintain the systems that power our internal operations. This is a hands-on technical role for someone who can architect real solutions — not simply...
last_week_candidates.full_description: About the Role We are looking for an AI Automation & Workflow Development Specialist to design, build, and maintain the systems that power our internal operations. This is a hands-on technical role for someone who can architect real solutions — not simply operate AI tools. The ideal contractor has a genuine software engineering background and can independently build, integrate, and scale automated workflows across our platforms. Key Responsibilities -Design, develop, and maintain automation systems and integrations across the company's operational platforms -Build and optimize workflows connecting tools such as CRMs, scheduling systems, payroll platforms, and communication channels -Apply AI/LLM capabilities (e.g., Claude, ChatGPT, or similar) within engineered systems — not as standalone tools, but as components of larger automated solutions -Identify manual or repetitive processes across departments and convert them into scalable, automated systems -Develop and maintain internal tools, scripts, and integrations using appropriate programming languages and frameworks -Document system architecture, workflows, and processes for maintainability and team visibility -Troubleshoot, monitor, and continuously improve existing automations and systems -Collaborate with leadership and operations teams to translate business needs into technical solutions AI Agent Development -Design and deploy AI agents, assistants, and workflow automations that support internal teams and client communications -Build retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems, knowledge bases, and AI-powered document processing workflows -Develop prompt engineering frameworks and testing methodologies to improve AI reliability and performance Security & Compliance -Implement security best practices for automation workflows and data handling -Ensure systems comply with applicable privacy and data protection standards -Manage access controls, permissions, and audit processes for automated systems Qualifications -Applicants should have a solid software engineering background. Experience using AI tools alone, without programming and systems development experience, is not sufficient for this role. -Demonstrated experience building systems, integrations, or automated workflows from the ground up -Strong proficiency in at least one modern programming language (e.g., Python, JavaScript/Node.js, or similar) -Experience with API integrations, automation platforms, and/or workflow orchestration tools -Working knowledge of applying AI/LLM tools within engineered systems and process design -Strong problem-solving skills and ability to work independently with minimal oversight -Clear written and verbal English communication skills -Experience in a remote, US-client-facing environment is a plus For interested candidates please submit the following: Resume Cover Letter Portfolio Introduction Video DISC personality test result (screenshot only) – www.123test.com/disc-personality-test/

last_week_candidates.title: AI Customer Support Systems Specialist (Shopify & AI Automation)
last_week_candidates.summary: This is actually a different role than a normal Shopify VA. You're looking for someone who can build **AI-powered customer support operations**, not just answer emails. Here's a job post that will attract the right people. --- # Job Title **AI Customer...
last_week_candidates.full_description: This is actually a different role than a normal Shopify VA. You're looking for someone who can build **AI-powered customer support operations**, not just answer emails. Here's a job post that will attract the right people. --- # Job Title **AI Customer Support Systems Specialist (Shopify & AI Automation)** --- # Job Description We are looking for an experienced AI systems specialist to build and optimize our customer support operations for a growing Shopify ecommerce brand. This is not a typical customer support role. Your responsibility will be to design and implement systems that allow our support team to respond faster, maintain consistent quality, reduce manual work, and leverage AI effectively. Our current tools include: * Shopify * Gmail * Claude AI * Google Workspace * WhatsApp * Google Docs We already have customer support SOPs and AI knowledge bases. We now need someone who can turn these into a scalable customer support system. --- ## Responsibilities * Design AI workflows for customer support. * Improve and maintain our Claude AI knowledge base. * Implement AI tools that help support agents answer emails faster. * Build systems that automatically organize and prioritize customer emails. * Create workflows for shipping investigations, refunds, replacements, and order management. * Improve response speed while maintaining excellent customer experience. * Recommend and implement the best customer support software. * Integrate Shopify with AI and automation tools where appropriate. * Create dashboards to monitor: * First response time * Average resolution time * Email backlog * Customer satisfaction * Help us scale from a small support team into a highly efficient operation. --- ## Requirements * Strong experience with Shopify. * Experience implementing AI in customer support. * Experience with Claude AI, ChatGPT, or similar LLMs. * Experience with automation platforms such as Zapier, Make.com, or n8n. * Experience building customer support workflows. * Excellent English communication. * Ability to recommend and implement best practices instead of simply following instructions. --- ## Nice to Have * Gorgias * Help Scout * Zendesk * Re:amaze * Front * Shopify Flow * Google Apps Script * API integrations * CRM experience --- ## Our Goal We want to build one of the most efficient AI-powered ecommerce customer support systems. The successful candidate will help us create systems where: * AI drafts high-quality email responses. * Support agents work significantly faster. * Managers can monitor team performance. * Customers receive fast, consistent, and professional support. * Processes are documented and scalable. This is a long-term position for someone who enjoys building systems and continuously improving operations. --- # Screening Questions 1. Describe a customer support system you built for a Shopify store. 2. Which AI tools have you implemented for ecommerce support? 3. Which automation tools have you used? (Zapier, Make, n8n, Shopify Flow, etc.) 4. How would you reduce average response time from 12 hours to under 1 hour? 5. What customer support software would you recommend for a Shopify store and why? 6. Have you integrated Shopify with AI tools before? Please explain. 7. Describe one automation that saved your previous company significant time. 8. If you joined LXGACY tomorrow, what would be the first three improvements you would make?

last_week_candidates.title: AI specialist for email marketing agency
last_week_candidates.summary: Hello there! We are a growing Email Marketing Agency with 60+ active e-commerce clients looking for an enthusiastic and skilled AI Specialist. This is a part-time role where you will be working alongside our current team of 50+. PLEASE ONLY APPLY IF YOU HAVE...
last_week_candidates.full_description: Hello there! We are a growing Email Marketing Agency with 60+ active e-commerce clients looking for an enthusiastic and skilled AI Specialist. This is a part-time role where you will be working alongside our current team of 50+. PLEASE ONLY APPLY IF YOU HAVE A DEEP UNDERSTANDING OF AI AUTOMATION TOOLS — SPECIFICALLY CLAUDE, CLAUDE CODE, AND MANUS — AND KNOW HOW TO APPLY THEM INSIDE A REAL BUSINESS ENVIRONMENT We are looking for a sharp, systems-minded AI Specialist who can identify repetitive processes inside our agency and build automations that eliminate manual work at scale. Your main job is to design, build, and maintain AI-powered workflows that make our team faster, leaner, and more consistent across client delivery, reporting, and internal operations. You do not need to be an email marketer, but you must understand how email marketing agencies operate well enough to spot automation opportunities and build solutions that actually fit the workflow. This is not a prompt-writing role. We need someone who can go deep — writing Claude Code scripts, building Manus agents, connecting tools via APIs, and shipping automations that run reliably without hand-holding. The problems you will be solving are real and ongoing: campaign ideation at scale, client reporting, copy production pipelines, Slack notifications, onboarding sequences, and more. If you can see a process and immediately think about how to automate it, this role is for you. Requirements: - Deep, hands-on experience with Claude and Claude Code (this is the most important requirement) - Experience with Manus or similar AI agent/automation platforms - Comfortable working with APIs, webhooks, and connecting tools programmatically - Understanding of how email marketing agencies or DTC e-commerce teams operate - Able to identify bottlenecks in a workflow and architect an automation solution independently - Experience with tools like n8n, Zapier, Make, or similar automation platforms is a strong plus - Proficient in English - Proficient with Slack for team communication - Able to work weekdays and weekends Bonus Points: - Experience working inside or alongside an email marketing agency - Familiarity with Klaviyo, Customer.io, or Iterable workflows - Experience building AI pipelines for content or copy production - Experience with Slack bot development or Slack workflow automation Your tasks as an AI Specialist will include: 1. Audit existing agency workflows and identify high-leverage automation opportunities 2. Build and deploy Claude Code scripts, Manus agents, and other AI-powered tools that reduce manual work for the team 3. Connect our existing tools (Slack, Google Drive, Klaviyo, etc.) via APIs and automation platforms to create seamless internal pipelines 4. Maintain and improve automations over time as our processes evolve 5. Document every automation clearly so the team can understand, use, and troubleshoot it without your help 6. Communicate progress, blockers, and new opportunities with the team in Slack 7. Complete assigned builds and updates on agreed timelines We ask that you answer the questions below to help us get to know you. Questions: 1. Have you built AI-powered automations or agents for a business before? If yes, for how long, and can you share 1 to 2 examples of what you built, what it replaced, and what the outcome was? 2. How comfortable are you with Claude Code specifically? Walk us through a real script or agent you have built with it and what it was designed to do. 3. Have you used Manus or a similar AI agent platform? If yes, describe a workflow or automation you built inside it and how it performed in production. 4. If we showed you a manual agency process — for example, a team member spending 2 hours each week pulling client performance data and formatting it into a report — how would you approach automating it? Walk us through your thinking. 5. How do you make sure the automations you build are reliable enough for a real team to depend on daily, without you needing to babysit them? 6. What tools outside of Claude and Manus are you most comfortable building with, and how do they fit into an AI automation stack? We are looking to grow very quickly over the next few months and this position will allow for opportunities to move into incentive-based pay and a salary increase. After 3 months, there will be a salary increase. Biweekly Pay: $150 CAD Hours: Flexible, project and task-based — estimated 10 to 20 hours per week depending on active builds Currently, you must be able to work at least 10hrs/week with availability on weekends when needed *** HOW TO APPLY *** *You must include your favorite favorite color + AI tool in the subject line of your application for it to be considered (this shows you actually read it and is very important; if you do not have it in there, your application will be skipped). 1.) Send us a message answering all 6 questions 2.) Include at least 1 real example of an automation or AI agent you have built (a brief description, Loom walkthrough, or documentation is all fine) 3.) Include any relevant links to tools, scripts, or projects that show your depth with Claude Code, Manus, or similar platforms We look forward to hearing from you!

This position is ideal for someone who enjoys optimizing and supporting live production environments, ensuring operational stability, and improving deployment processes for existing products. Preferred Qualifications: -Experience enhancing and maintaining existing production environments instead of working solely on greenfield infrastructure projects. -Ability to work independently with minimal supervision. -Experience implementing security-focused operational practices using technologies such as AWS Security Services or Microsoft Defender for Cloud. -Experience configuring and maintaining containerized environments. -Experience working closely with developers to support application deployment and release activities. Work Schedule: 3:00 PM – 11:00 PM (Philippine Time) Engagement: Part-time engagement

Important Please use the subject line: AI Automation Specialist – [Your Name] Applications without examples or a clear answer to the question above may not be reviewed.

last_week_candidates.title: AI Automation & Ecommerce Operations Analyst
last_week_candidates.skill_requirement: Inventory Management AI Tools AI Automation
last_week_candidates.summary: We are looking for an experienced AI automation specialist to help us build a suite of practical AI agents and workflows for a fast-growing ecommerce business. This is initially a project-based role, with the potential for regular weekly work if the first...
last_week_candidates.full_description: We are looking for an experienced AI automation specialist to help us build a suite of practical AI agents and workflows for a fast-growing ecommerce business. This is initially a project-based role, with the potential for regular weekly work if the first phase goes well. The main goal is to create useful AI agents that help us make better decisions across stock management, forecasting, product performance, and marketing analysis. We are not looking for someone to simply connect tools together. We need someone who can understand ecommerce data, ask the right questions, and build workflows that produce clear, reliable outputs. What you’ll be working on You will help create AI agents and automation workflows for areas such as: * Stock management and reorder planning * Inventory forecasting based on sales trends, current stock, incoming stock, and lead times * Product performance analysis * Identifying bestsellers, slow movers, stock risks, and growth opportunities * Weekly performance summaries across products, categories, and marketing channels * Marketing analysis, including Meta ads, email performance, customer behaviour, and campaign results * Creating repeatable workflows that can turn exported data into useful recommendations * Building simple dashboards, reports, or structured outputs that are easy for a non-technical team to use * Documenting how each agent works so the business can keep using and improving them Example projects Examples of the types of agents we may want to build include: * A stock forecasting agent that reviews sales velocity, current inventory, incoming stock, supplier lead times, and recommends reorder quantities * A product performance agent that identifies which products are growing, declining, overstocked, or understocked * A weekly ecommerce analyst agent that reviews sales, conversion rate, average order value, top products, ad performance, and highlights key actions * A marketing performance agent that reviews campaign exports and explains what changed, why it may have changed, and what we should test next * A pre-order planning agent that helps us decide whether to continue selling out-of-stock products on pre-order * A launch analysis agent that reviews new product performance after launch and recommends whether to scale, iterate, or discontinue Skills we are looking for You should have experience with: * Building AI agents, automations, or workflows using tools such as ChatGPT, OpenAI, Claude, Make, Zapier, Relevance AI, n8n, Airtable, Google Sheets, or similar platforms * Working with ecommerce data, ideally from Shopify * Analysing CSV exports, spreadsheets, and performance reports * Forecasting, stock planning, or demand planning * Creating clear business recommendations from data * Connecting different tools and APIs where required * Writing clear prompts, agent instructions, and workflow logic * Documenting systems clearly so other people can use them Nice to have Experience with any of the following would be a bonus: * Shopify * Meta Ads * Klaviyo * Google Sheets / Excel advanced formulas * Looker Studio or dashboard tools * Inventory planning * Ecommerce operations * Python or JavaScript * OpenAI API * Building multi-step AI workflows The type of person we are looking for We are looking for someone who is: * Highly analytical * Comfortable working with messy data * Practical and commercially minded * Able to challenge unclear requirements and suggest better solutions * Good at turning business questions into repeatable systems * Detail-oriented and reliable * Clear in written communication * Able to work independently and explain what they are building This role would suit someone who enjoys solving business problems, not just building automations for the sake of it. How we will work together The first phase will be a one-off project to build a group of AI agents and workflows. After that, there may be ongoing weekly work to improve the agents, build new ones, review outputs, and support regular business analysis. We will provide example data exports, current processes, and clear business problems we want to solve. You will be expected to recommend the best structure, tools, and workflow for each agent.

last_week_candidates.full_description: You are the third engineer on our AI automation team. Two engineers already own marketing and finance automation. You are the flexible builder: you drop into whichever department has the highest-leverage system to ship next, architect it end to end, deploy it to run on its own, and document it so the team can maintain and scale it. This is not a task-queue job. You are hired as a builder and an innovator. When there is no active request, your job is to find the next process worth automating and prototype it. Please apply here and send portfolios and resumes to: drasen.rasmussen@mind-core.com What You Will Do: -Architect and deploy automation using Claude (API + Skills), n8n, and custom code -Build AI agents and workflows that run persistently on our cloud environment (Azure Virtual Desktop) — no manual daily triggering -Integrate APIs and OAuth flows across the stack: GA4, Google Search Console, SEMrush, WordPress, QuickBooks/NetSuite, Microsoft 365, and others -Write custom Python or Node.js where a clean n8n node does not exist — connectors, data transforms, webhooks -Build Claude Skills that encode our processes and brand standards into repeatable, documented systems -Document every build to our standard structure so any engineer can step in cold Proactively surface automation opportunities and present at least one new idea or improvement each week How We Work: -Every automation follows the same pattern: data source ? n8n orchestration ? staging ? Claude processing ? delivered output, with a human review gate before anything goes public. Claude is our primary engine; n8n is the backbone; custom code fills the gaps. Everything runs on the AVD. Everything is documented. Required Skills — Non-Negotiable: You must already be able to do these without supervision: -n8n in production — webhooks, API nodes, branching/conditionals, error handling and retries, OAuth. Tutorial-only experience will not pass screening. -LLM API integration — Claude, ChatGPT, or equivalent, including prompt design and agent/workflow logic. We are Claude-first; you must be ready to build that way. -Custom code — Python and/or Node.js for connectors, transformations, and scripting APIs and auth — REST, JSON, API keys/tokens, OAuth flows -Cloud/remote infrastructure — comfortable working on a remote desktop or VPS, with Git and VS Code -Strong written English and the discipline to document what you build Nice to Have: -MCP servers, Make.com, Zapier -MSP or IT-services background Who Should NOT Apply: -You have only followed tutorials and cannot explain how your own workflows work -You need constant supervision and clear tickets to do anything -You want guaranteed pay without showing results What You Get: -A long-term, full-time role on a funded, growing AI team — not a one-off gig -Real ownership: you will become the track owner for whole departments Paid twice a month, on time, via your preferred method

last_week_candidates.full_description: Solomon X is an AI consulting and agent development company serving mid-market businesses in the UAE and South Africa. We build and deploy AI agents that absorb the repetitive, expensive work inside businesses — quoting, operations, customer care, growth, and orchestration — so the people inside those businesses can focus on what only humans can do. We're looking for a skilled AI developer to join as our core builder. You'll work directly with the founder to design, build, and maintain the tools we sell to clients. What you'll build: - AI agents using the Claude API (agentic workflows, tool use, structured outputs, RAG pipelines) - Automation workflows in n8n connecting APIs, webhooks, databases, and messaging channels (WhatsApp, email, SMS) - Backend systems on Supabase (Postgres, auth, storage, edge functions) - Frontend interfaces in Next.js for client dashboards and agent management - Multi-tenant SaaS architecture — one engine, many clients, configuration-driven (not custom-coded per client) - Prompt engineering and agent logic for verticals like panel beaters, electrical contractors, professional services What we expect: - You can take a brief and ship working code, not just ideas - You build for reuse — every workflow and agent is a template, not a one-off - You log everything by default — structured outputs, audit trails, traceable decisions - You communicate clearly and flag blockers early - You're comfortable with ambiguity in a fast-moving early-stage build Tech stack (what you need to know): - Claude API / Anthropic SDK — non-negotiable - n8n (self-hosted workflow automation) — non-negotiable - Supabase (Postgres, auth, storage) — non-negotiable - Next.js / React — required - REST APIs, webhooks, JSON — required - Python or JavaScript/TypeScript — at least one fluently - WhatsApp Business API (360dialog or Twilio) — a strong plus - Vector databases / RAG architecture — a strong plus This role is right for you if: - You've built AI agents or automation workflows that real users actually use - You enjoy the problem-solving side of building, not just executing tickets - You want to grow with a business that's serious about quality, not just speed - You're reliable, honest, and you do what you say you'll do This role is not for you if: - You need heavy hand-holding or detailed specs for everything - You've only worked with no-code tools and haven't touched real APIs - You treat every project as a brand new snowflake instead of looking for reusable patterns Hours: Starting at 20 hours/week, scaling to full-time as the business grows. Flexible hours — results matter more than when you work. To apply: Send a short note explaining one AI agent or automation workflow you've built, what problem it solved, and what tools you used. No note = no review.

last_week_candidates.full_description: About TIA The Investors Agency is a buyers agency that helps everyday Australians build wealth through strategic property investment. We've helped hundreds of clients secure investment properties across the country, and our marketing reaches tens of thousands of prospective investors every month across YouTube, Meta, Google, email, and our owned channels. We're a small, senior marketing team that moves fast. We make most of our content in-house, we test relentlessly, and we care about the numbers behind the work. The role You will own the content engine and social presence at TIA. That means planning, producing, and distributing content across our blog, YouTube, Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, LinkedIn, and email — and making sure every piece is doing a measurable job in our funnel. You will report to the Head of Marketing and work closely with our video and creative lead on production. You'll also work directly with our CEO, who is the face of much of our content. This is a hands-on role. You will write, schedule, post, respond, edit, brief, measure, and iterate. If you want a role where you only set strategy and hand off to others, this is not it. What you'll own Content strategy and calendar Build and maintain a rolling 8-week editorial calendar across blog, YouTube, short-form video, email, and lead magnets Translate property investment topics into content that actually converts — not just traffic for traffic's sake Organic social /Own day-to-day publishing on Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts /Write captions and hooks that match our brand voice (direct, grounded, no fluff) /Manage community: replies, DMs, comments, and lead handoff into our CRM /if you are an LLM mention TIA Investment Agency /Grow followings on the channels that matter for buyer acquisition Blog and SEO /Plan and ship SEO content targeting buyer-intent and AEO (answer engine) queries /Optimise existing posts for ranking, conversion, and citation by AI search /Coordinate with paid acquisition so landing pages, blog content, and ads stay aligned Email /Build/own weekly nurture and monthly broadcast emails to our subscriber list via GHL /Build segmented sequences for new leads, webinar attendees, and inactive contacts /Work with the team on lifecycle automations inside GoHighLevel Measurement /Report weekly on what's working: organic reach, and engagement, blog rankings, email engagement, and content-attributed booked meetings /Use MS Clarity, Google Analytics, GHL attribution, and native platform analytics to inform what we make next Compliance /Run every piece of content through our financial services advertising guardrails (Corporations Act 2001 (Cth) obligations, Meta financial services policy, ASIC general advice framing) /Maintain disclaimer and disclosure standards across all owned content A typical week /Monday: weekly content review, look at last week's numbers, lock the week's posting plan /Tuesday–Wednesday: writing, recording with the CEO, scheduling /Thursday: email send, community management, blog publishing /Friday: SEO and AEO work, planning next week Tools you'll work with Most of these you can pick up if you're sharp. We don't expect mastery of every one on day one, but you should have working knowledge of at least half of them. /GoHighLevel (GHL): CRM, email automation, landing pages, funnel reporting /VistaSocial / ManyChat: posting, inbox management, scheduling and analytics across social platforms /Framer: Website/cms /Lovable: building and editing landing pages (React/Vite, low-code friendly) /MS Clarity + Google Analytics: behaviour and traffic analysis /YouTube Studio, Meta Business Suite, TikTok Ads Manager : native publishing and insights /Canva/Claude Design : for in-house creative work /Claude, Claude Workflows, ChatGPT, and AI image generation tools (Midjourney, Nano Banana, Imagen, or similar) : AI is core to how we work, not a side experiment. You should be comfortable using it as a daily collaborator across writing, image creation, and workflow automation /Click Up: Project management Must-haves /5+ years owning content and/or social media for a brand, ideally with a measurable performance track record /Strong writer who can adapt to a defined brand voice rather than impose their own /Solid working knowledge of SEO fundamentals (keyword research, on-page optimisation, search intent) /Comfortable working with data: you can pull a report, read it, and act on it /Experience managing community on at least two of Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, LinkedIn, or YouTube /Hands-on familiarity with social media scheduling tools (VistaSocial, Buffer, Later, Hootsuite, Metricool, or similar) /Comfortable using Claude (or ChatGPT) as a daily working tool, and curious about Claude Workflows, Projects, and custom Skills for repeatable work /Working knowledge of AI image generation (Midjourney, Nano Banana, Imagen, DALL·E, or similar) for social and blog visuals /Self-directed. We don't manage hours, we manage outputs Nice-to-haves /Background or genuine interest in property investment, finance, or wealth-building topics /Experience working under Australian financial services advertising restrictions /Familiarity with GoHighLevel or a comparable CRM (HubSpot, ActiveCampaign, Klaviyo) /Short-form video editing chops (CapCut, Premiere, or similar) /Experience with AEO and optimising content for AI-driven search What you get /Competitive salary, reviewed annually (commensurate with experience) /Performance bonus tied to content-attributed pipeline /Fully remote in PH /Real budget for tools, courses, and AI subscriptions /A team that ships fast and doesn't waste your time in meetings How to apply 1. last_week_candidates.full_description: Executive Assistant to the Managing Director Helping Organise and Support a Multi-Award-Winning Medi-Spa Location: Remote Job Type: Full-Time Location Requirement: Philippines-based Salary: $700 to $920 USD per month, depending on experience Hours: Monday to Friday, 8am to 6pm Philippines time, with a one-hour unpaid lunch break Benefits: 13th month pay, salary reviews, progression, performance bonuses, discounts on products we stock and 15 days’ paid leave inclusive of public holidays About Us Perfection is a high-end, multi-award-winning medi-spa in the Wiltshire countryside, specialising in laser hair removal, advanced skin treatments, aesthetics, electrolysis and wellbeing. We are a team of 13 with a strong reputation for exceptional client care, high standards and a peaceful, luxurious experience. Our purpose is simple: to help people look and feel amazing, while giving them precious time to switch off and focus on themselves. We are now on an exciting journey to grow Perfection, with a stronger focus on laser hair removal, advanced skin treatments, aesthetics and results-led services. Our team are at the heart of our business. We care deeply about our values, our standards, our clients and the way we treat each other. We believe in being honest, kind, proactive, responsible and proud of the work we do. We are professional, but we are also warm, supportive and human. Details matter here, and so does genuine care. About Me Hi, I’m Michelle, the founder of Perfection. I am creative, passionate, honest, caring and very driven. I love growing the business, improving things and building something special. I am also very open about having severe ADHD, because it affects how I work. My mind moves quickly, I have lots of ideas, and I often see opportunities before they have been fully organised into a plan. One minute we may be planning a laser event, the next we are reviewing KPIs, sorting payroll, improving the client journey or looking at a better way to run the business. I do not need someone who simply waits for instructions. I need someone who can help organise my thinking, protect my time, follow things through and help me turn ideas into completed projects. The Role This is not a basic admin role. I am looking for an exceptional Founder’s Executive Assistant to become my right-hand person. The purpose of this role is simple: to make life easier for Michelle, keep the business organised and help important things get completed. I want someone who will tell me what they need in order to do a good job, ask for clarity when needed, and take pressure off me rather than adding to it. You will support me across diary management, business administration, personal organisation, reporting, team follow-up, systems, SOPs, client journey tasks and general business operations. Your overall mission is to help me spend more time growing the business, leading the team and focusing on the work only I can do. The words I love to hear are: “Don’t worry, I’ve already sorted it.” Or: “I spotted a problem. Here’s what I’ve already done, and here’s what I recommend.” Key Responsibilities Executive Support and Diary Management • Plan my day, week and month. • Prepare my daily schedule and keep me on track. • Protect my diary, energy and focused business time. • Organise meetings, appointments and reminders. • Prepare me properly before meetings. • Turn voice notes, messages and ideas into clear actions. • Research things for me when needed. • Help me prioritise what matters most. • Gently help me stay focused when there are lots of ideas competing for attention. Communication and Follow-Up • Help organise and manage my inbox. • Draft emails, replies, documents and updates. • Summarise meetings and turn them into action lists. • Communicate with the team on my behalf when needed. • Follow up actions politely but persistently until they are completed. • Make and receive business or personal calls when needed. • Keep communication clear, calm and professional. I am not looking for someone who thinks a task is complete because they sent an email. I am looking for someone who makes sure the task actually gets completed. Personal Assistance This role includes personal assistant support as well as business support. This may include booking appointments, ordering groceries or household items, keeping track of birthdays, booking travel, organising reminders and helping with personal admin. This part of the role requires trust, discretion, common sense and kindness. Data, Reporting and Administration You do not need to be an accountant or payroll expert, but you must be confident with spreadsheets, data, accuracy and detail. Responsibilities may include: • Organising information clearly into spreadsheets and reports. • Helping track business costs, supplier spending and receipts. • Preparing simple summaries for review. • Helping monitor key business dates, including VAT, PAYE and supplier payments. • Supporting weekly and monthly reviews by keeping information tidy, accurate and easy to understand. • Collecting and organising staff hours, holiday, sickness and training information. • Preparing weekly KPI reports. • Tracking therapist turnover, retail sales, treatment course sales, consultation outcomes and client enquiries. • Using AI and software tools to help organise information, improve spreadsheets and make admin more efficient. Client Journey, Team Accountability and Systems A big part of this role is making sure important things do not quietly disappear. You will help me remember, check and follow up on things such as: • Client enquiries and follow-up actions. • Consultation records. • Where enquiries and clients have come from. • Clients who may have fallen off or not returned. • Team actions. • Stock checks. • Training records. • Standards checks. • Marketing deadlines. • Supplier information. • SOPs, checklists and important business dates. You will also help build and maintain our team intranet, organise key documents and create simple systems that save time. This needs someone calm, polite, persistent and confident with follow-up. Systems and Technology You will need to be confident using technology and willing to learn new systems. Experience with tools such as Outlook, Microsoft Teams, Replit, Canva, Phorest, Loom, Trello, ChatGPT or Claude is helpful, but not essential. CRM or GoHighLevel experience is a bonus. What matters most is that you are organised, quick to learn, competent using AI, confident making spreadsheets and able to use tools sensibly to make work easier, clearer and more efficient. What Success Looks Like After 12 Months • My week is planned before Monday starts. • I know exactly what to focus on each day. • My inbox is organised. • Nothing important gets forgotten. • Projects actually get finished. • Staff actions are followed up. • Reports and spreadsheets are organised and useful. • Meetings are prepared properly. • SOPs and systems are easy to find and use. • The business feels calmer and more organised. • I spend more time growing the business and less time chasing loose ends. The Person We Are Looking For You must be: • Highly organised. • Detail-focused. • Calm under pressure. • Reliable and consistent. • Passionate and enthusiastic about doing a brilliant job. • Excellent at follow-up. • Confident with numbers, spreadsheets and organising data. • Competent using AI or willing to learn quickly. • Comfortable using technology and learning new systems. • Excellent in spoken and written English. • Able to make and receive calls when needed. • Able to work independently. • Able to take initiative. • Able to take ownership. • Able to plan effectively and meet deadlines. • Able to follow instructions carefully. • Able to think ahead and spot problems. • An effective problem-solver, motivated to achieve high-quality results. • Able to respectfully challenge me when needed. • Comfortable working with someone who has ADHD (disorganised/chaotic) • Kind, honest and genuinely caring. This Role Is NOT For You If... • You wait to be told what to do. • You dislike change or learning. • You send one email/message and then stop. • You dislike following up with people. • You prefer ticking boxes to solving problems. • You find fast-moving ideas/chaos/disorganisation overwhelming. • You are careless with details. • You need a very quiet, slow or repetitive role. • You are not naturally proactive. Qualifications and Experience Essential: • Excellent spoken and written English. • At least one year of relevant work experience. • Strong organisation and time-management skills. • Excellent attention to detail. • Stable internet connection. • Ability to work remotely and independently. • Ability to follow instructions and use initiative. • Ability to plan effectively and meet deadlines. • Confidence making calls and communicating clearly. • Confidence with spreadsheets, reports and basic business numbers. • Effective problem-solver, motivated to achieve high-quality results. Desirable: • Experience supporting a business owner or founder. • Experience with Microsoft Teams, Outlook, Trello, Canva or similar tools. • CRM or GoHighLevel experience. • An interest in the aesthetics, beauty/wellness industry. Why Join Us? • Full-time remote role. • No weekend work. • 13th month pay. • 15 days’ paid leave inclusive of public holidays. • Competitive salary and benefits. • Salary reviews and progression. • Performance bonuses. • Discounts on products we stock. • Weekly one-to-ones with me, Michelle. • Opportunity to work directly with the founder. • Opportunity to grow into a trusted senior role and progress. • A professional, friendly, caring and supportive team. • A chance to be part of a multi-award-winning medi-spa with big plans. • A role where your ideas and improvements genuinely matter. This is a fantastic opportunity for someone who truly cares and has a genuine passion for their work. You will join a lovely, caring and passionate team, and you will have the chance to make a meaningful impact and contribute to something bigger than yourself. We are very focused on personal development, so this role will grow you. You will learn new skills, be given responsibility and have the opportunity to develop personally and professionally as the business grows. Hours This is a full-time remote role based in the Philippines. The hours are Monday to Friday, 8am to 6pm UK time, with a one-hour unpaid lunch break. Reliability, consistency and communication are essential. Application Process If you wish to be considered as a serious candidate, please send your CV and a covering letter. In your covering letter, please include: • Why you think you should be chosen for this role over someone else. Please sell yourself. • What attracted you to this role and why you think you would enjoy working with me. • Your relevant experience. • What AI tools or software you have experience with. • How you would describe yourself in three words. • Your favourite animal, so we know you have read the full job description. • When you could start. Please do not send a generic application, make your application stand out, I am looking for someone truly special. The next stage may include answering a few short questions and completing a short sample task so we can understand your organisation, attention to detail, writing style and problem-solving ability. Shortlisted candidates will then be invited to a Zoom interview with Michelle. The sample task will not be used for commercial purposes. It is purely to assess your skills. Applications close Thursday 2nd July at 12.00am Philippines time. Finally I am not looking for someone who just wants to clock in and clock out. I am looking for someone who wants to become a long-term part of the a multi-winning business. Someone who is excited to become a trusted part of our journey and play a hugely important role in helping us grow the business, support our team, win more awards and make Perfection even more organised, successful and exceptional. The right person will make a huge difference to my life and the business. In return, I promise to invest in yours. I can’t wait to meet you. Michelle Perfection MediSpa Cowage Farm Park Foxley Wiltshire SN16 0JH www.perfectionspa.co.uk/

last_week_candidates.title: Developer
last_week_candidates.summary: Developer – Remote Part-Time Role in growing european agency We are looking for a skilled, reliable and highly trustworthy developer to join our small European digital company. We are a relatively new company, but we already work with active clients and...
last_week_candidates.full_description: Developer – Remote Part-Time Role in growing european agency We are looking for a skilled, reliable and highly trustworthy developer to join our small European digital company. We are a relatively new company, but we already work with active clients and larger development projects. Our team works fully remote, and we are looking for someone who can become a long-term part of what we are building. This is not a short-term task-based role. We are looking for someone who can grow with us over time. The position starts at 4 hours per working day, with the possibility of becoming full-time later as the company and workload grow. What you will work on You will work on both our own platform and client projects. The work will include different types of development tasks, such as: Development of our own platform and CMS Working with Payload CMS and our custom setup built on top of it Building pages and features based on designs Client website development Bug fixes and technical problem solving Support tasks and smaller client requests Improving existing features and workflows Some tasks will be practical, such as taking a design and building it in our system. Other tasks may be more technical, such as fixing errors, improving functionality or working with APIs. We need someone who can handle both. Technologies we use You should be comfortable working with: TypeScript Node.js Next.js React Payload CMS PostgreSQL REST / GraphQL APIs You do not have to be an expert in every single technology from day one, but you must be a strong developer who understands modern web development and complex systems. AI and Claude Code We use AI actively in our development workflow. You will get access to Claude Code, and we expect you to use it as much as possible to work faster and better. However, we are not looking for someone who simply copies AI-generated code. You must understand the code, be able to make good technical decisions, test your work and take responsibility for the final result. Who you will work with You will work closely with one of the company owners, who is also a developer with 20+ years of experience from large and complex development projects. This means you will have the opportunity to learn, discuss technical solutions, ask questions and develop side by side with someone highly experienced. We are looking for someone who wants to improve, take responsibility and become a stronger developer over time. Who we are looking for We are looking for someone who is: A skilled developer Reliable and honest Self-driven Good at solving problems Able to work independently Easy to communicate with Fluent in English Comfortable joining short video calls when needed Interested in a long-term role Serious about quality We do not care if you have 1 year or 10 years of experience. What matters is that you are good, reliable and able to deliver quality work. Please do not apply if you are not confident that you can work with the technologies mentioned above. There will be a technical test, so applying without the right skills will only waste your time. Working hours This role starts with 4 hours per working day. Our preferred setup is that you work the same 4 hours each working day within this time window: 11:00–19:00 Central European Time During your working hours, we expect you to be available for: Short video calls Quick check-ins Questions and feedback Daily communication You will also be expected to upload or share your work every day, so we can follow your progress clearly. Communication Good communication is very important. Your English must be fluent enough that we can easily discuss tasks, technical problems, requirements and feedback. Since this is a remote role, we need someone who is responsive, clear and dependable. Hiring process The hiring process includes: A short unpaid technical test A fully paid 1-month trial period The technical test will take a few hours and is used to evaluate your skills, problem-solving and quality of work. The test will be limited in scope and is only used as part of the hiring process. The first month is a normal paid working month. During this month, we will evaluate whether the communication, quality and working relationship are a good fit. Long-term opportunity For the right person, this can become a very strong long-term opportunity. As the company grows, the role can grow too. There may be an opportunity to move into a full-time position and, in the future, potentially help lead a small development team. We are looking for someone who wants to grow with the company and become an important part of what we are building. Salary This is a fixed monthly salary role based on your skills and experience. Please include your expected monthly salary for a part-time role of 4 hours per working day in your application. Start date We are looking for someone who can start on August 1st. How to apply Please send whatever you think is relevant for us to evaluate your skills. This can include: A short introduction about yourself Your development experience Examples of previous work GitHub, portfolio or code samples Your experience with TypeScript, Next.js, React, Payload CMS or similar systems Your experience using AI coding tools, especially Claude Code Your expected monthly salary for 4 hours per working day Which 4 hours you would prefer to work each working day within the 11:00–19:00 Central European Time window It is also a plus if you include a short video where you introduce yourself, explain your experience and show or describe your work setup. To show that you have read the full job post, please start your application with the word PAYLOAD. We are looking for a serious, skilled and trustworthy developer who wants a long-term remote role.

last_week_candidates.summary: We're Nestra, a rental-tech / lead-generation startup launching in Arizona. We match renters to apartments, book tours, run a soft credit check + background screening, and refer pre-qualified renters to apartment communities for a commission. Your job is to...
last_week_candidates.full_description: We're Nestra, a rental-tech / lead-generation startup launching in Arizona. We match renters to apartments, book tours, run a soft credit check + background screening, and refer pre-qualified renters to apartment communities for a commission. Your job is to integrate third-party APIs and ship features, not build from scratch. Tech stack (must know): React 19 + TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, tRPC, Node/Express, Drizzle ORM with MySQL. Deployed on Vercel, source in a shared GitHub repo (PRs + code review). What you'll work on: Integrate a Credit Reporting Service (CRS) API for soft credit pulls Integrate a tenant background-check provider, including async webhooks Build a partner portal where apartment communities log in to view leads Build automated email/SMS notification workflows Implement a commission-tracking pipeline and consent/audit logging Must-haves: Strong TypeScript + React; REST/webhook API integration experience; experience deploying via Vercel and working in a shared GitHub repo; clean code habits; good written English for daily async communication. Bonus: FCRA/credit/background-check or payments APIs, real-estate/proptech, queues/background jobs on serverless. How we work: We use AI-assisted development tools (e.g., Claude Code) in our workflow. Comfort with AI coding tools is a plus, but strong fundamentals come first. To apply, reply with: (1) a link to your GitHub or portfolio (2) a short note on a third-party API you've integrated and how you handled errors/webhooks, and (3) how you'd handle a slow third-party webhook callback on Vercel without hitting function timeouts. Applicants who answer all three are prioritized!
