last_week_candidates.summary: # Full-Time Project Manager for Web Design and SEO Agency ## About Frisco Web Agency Frisco Web Agency is a growing web design and digital marketing agency based in Frisco, Texas. Our primary service is designing and developing custom websites for...
last_week_candidates.full_description: # Full-Time Project Manager for Web Design and SEO Agency ## About Frisco Web Agency Frisco Web Agency is a growing web design and digital marketing agency based in Frisco, Texas. Our primary service is designing and developing custom websites for service-based businesses. We also manage ongoing Search Engine Optimization campaigns and occasionally take on other digital projects. We are currently a small team of three: * Christopher Potter — Owner * Rocky Diana — Web Developer * Melissa Saldana — Administrative Support We are looking for a full-time Project Manager who can take ownership of our client projects, organize our fulfillment process, and keep everything moving forward without requiring constant direction. ## The Main Outcome of This Role The most important responsibility of this position is simple: **Make sure every project continues moving forward and is completed on time—or preferably ahead of schedule.** As the owner, Christopher needs to focus more of his time on marketing, sales, strategy, and growing the company. Right now, managing multiple active projects can pull him away from those responsibilities. Your role will be to take ownership of the day-to-day management of our fulfillment. You will need to know: * Where every project currently stands * What has already been completed * What still needs to be completed * Who is responsible for each task * What is due next * What is falling behind * What information we are waiting on * What the client needs to know * What could prevent us from meeting a deadline We do not want someone who only updates tasks after being told what to do. We need someone who thinks ahead, identifies problems early, communicates clearly, and actively pushes projects toward completion every day. ## Primary Responsibilities ### Manage Website Projects Most of your work will involve managing custom website design and development projects. You will be responsible for: * Setting up and organizing projects in ClickUp * Reviewing each active project every day * Creating and assigning tasks * Confirming that tasks have clear descriptions, deadlines, and owners * Following up with team members about incomplete or overdue work * Identifying the next steps before the current steps are completed * Coordinating design, content, development, revisions, quality assurance, and launch * Gathering missing information and materials from clients * Communicating project updates to clients * Preventing projects from becoming stalled * Keeping projects on schedule and pushing for early completion * Escalating problems before they become emergencies ### Manage SEO Campaigns Website projects will be the primary responsibility, but you will also help manage ongoing SEO campaigns. This may include: * Organizing monthly SEO deliverables in ClickUp * Confirming that assigned SEO work is completed * Coordinating content, website updates, keyword research, on-page optimization, and reporting * Tracking recurring monthly responsibilities * Preparing client updates * Following up on missing approvals or information * Making sure monthly deliverables do not get overlooked You do not need to be an advanced SEO strategist, but you should be willing to learn our process and understand enough to manage the work effectively. ### Communicate With Clients You will become one of the main points of contact for active client projects. Communication may take place through: * Email * ClickUp * Google Meet * Internal team messages You will be responsible for: * Providing clear and proactive updates * Asking clients for missing content, feedback, approvals, or access * Explaining what is happening next * Following up when a client has not responded * Setting realistic expectations * Keeping communication professional, organized, and easy to understand * Making sure clients do not have to ask us repeatedly for updates Clients should feel that their project is organized, actively managed, and moving forward. ### Communicate With the Team You will communicate with our team throughout the workday, Monday through Friday. This includes: * Reviewing progress with team members * Following up on tasks * Answering project-related questions * Clarifying priorities * Identifying blockers * Confirming deadlines * Making sure team members understand what is expected * Communicating important client feedback * Helping the team determine what should be worked on next You must be comfortable holding people accountable respectfully and directly. ### Own and Improve ClickUp ClickUp is the primary project management system used by Frisco Web Agency. You will be expected to: * Keep ClickUp accurate and up to date * Make sure tasks are assigned correctly * Maintain deadlines, statuses, dependencies, and priorities * Ensure task descriptions contain enough information for the work to be completed * Keep client and internal communication organized * Improve dashboards, templates, automations, and workflows * Identify areas where ClickUp is becoming confusing or inefficient * Recommend and implement improvements * Make ClickUp a reliable source of truth for the entire company At any time, we should be able to open ClickUp and quickly understand the status of every project. ### Maintain Company Playbooks We use the Buy Back Your Time playbook method to document how work should be completed. You will help: * Create playbooks for repeatable processes * Update playbooks when our processes change * Organize instructions, videos, links, and resources * Identify missing documentation * Make sure team members follow the documented process * Improve processes based on problems that occur during fulfillment Our goal is to build a company that does not depend on information being stored only in one person’s head. ## What We Are Looking For The right person for this role is: * Highly organized * Proactive * Dependable * Detail-oriented * Comfortable following up with people * Able to communicate professionally with clients * Able to manage several projects at the same time * Calm when priorities change * Willing to take ownership of outcomes * Able to think several steps ahead * Comfortable asking questions when something is unclear * Able to make decisions without needing constant approval * Interested in improving systems and processes * Consistent with communication * Able to identify potential delays before deadlines are missed You should naturally notice when something has not been completed and take action without waiting to be reminded. ## This Role Is Not a Good Fit If You: * Wait for someone to tell you every next step * Avoid difficult conversations or follow-ups * Regularly forget details * Are uncomfortable holding others accountable * Only update ClickUp when someone reminds you * Become overwhelmed when managing several projects * Disappear during your working hours * Allow unanswered questions to sit unresolved * Assume that someone else will take responsibility * Communicate vaguely or leave people uncertain * Prefer completing isolated tasks instead of owning the overall outcome ## Required Qualifications * Previous experience in project management, operations, or client coordination * Experience using ClickUp or a similar project management platform * Strong written and spoken English * Strong organizational and time-management skills * Experience communicating with clients through email and meetings * Reliable computer and internet connection * Ability to work independently * Ability to manage multiple deadlines and projects * Ability to document and improve processes * Willingness to learn website development and SEO fulfillment processes Experience working for a web design, marketing, SEO, or digital agency is strongly preferred. ## Working Hours and Availability This is a full-time position requiring 40 hours per week, Monday through Friday. You must be available for a daily meeting with Christopher at: **8:00 a.m. Central Time, Monday through Friday** Because this is a communication-heavy role, you must also be responsive and available during your agreed working hours. Your working schedule will be established so that you have enough overlap with Christopher, the team, and our clients. This is not a position where you can complete all your work without communicating with the team. Active communication throughout the workweek is an essential part of the job. ## Trial Period and Payment This position will begin with a 90-day trial period. During the trial period, we will evaluate: * Reliability * Communication * Organization * Ownership * Ability to manage deadlines * ClickUp management * Client communication * Team coordination * Ability to keep projects moving * Overall fit with the company You will be paid every two weeks. You will complete each two-week work period and then receive payment for that completed period. **Starting compensation: [INSERT MONTHLY SALARY OR HOURLY RATE]** There is room for increased responsibility, compensation, and long-term growth as Frisco Web Agency grows. ## What Success Looks Like You will be successful in this role when: * Christopher no longer has to personally manage every project * Every project has a clear status, owner, deadline, and next step * Team members know what they should be working on * Clients receive proactive communication * Delays are identified and addressed early * ClickUp is consistently accurate * Tasks do not disappear or remain forgotten * Projects are completed on time or ahead of schedule * Company processes are documented and continuously improved * You can clearly explain the status of every active project without confusion or uncertainty The long-term goal is for you to fully own project fulfillment so Christopher can focus on marketing, sales, leadership, and growing the business. ## How to Apply Please include the following information in your application: 1. A short introduction about yourself 2. Your previous project management experience 3. Your experience using ClickUp 4. Any experience managing website or SEO projects 5. An example of a project that was falling behind and how you moved it forward 6. Your approach to following up with a team member who repeatedly misses deadlines 7. Your internet speed and computer specifications 8. Confirmation that you can attend a daily meeting at 8:00 a.m. Central Time 9. Your expected monthly salary 10. A short video introduction explaining why you believe you would be a strong fit for this position At the beginning of your application, include the words: **“I push projects forward.”** Applications that do not include this phrase will not be considered.

last_week_candidates.full_description: About Us We are building multiple high growth consumer brands and are looking for someone who wants to become a long-term member of our team rather than just completing oneoff projects. Our first business is Win Your PIP, a UK-based service helping people complete Personal Independence Payment (PIP) applications through a professionally written personalised answer service. Our second business is a direct to consumer e-commerce brand launching premium foot care products on Shopify. We're looking for someone who enjoys improving websites, building landing pages, increasing conversion rates and using data to grow online businesses. Your Role This is not a traditional web design role. Your primary responsibility is increasing enquiries, sales and conversion rates. You will continually improve our websites using customer behaviour, analytics and testing. Responsibilities Optimise WordPress landing pages for higher conversion rates. Build and optimise Shopify product pages. Improve page speed and mobile experience. Create new landing pages for advertising campaigns. Implement Meta Pixel, Google Analytics 4 and conversion tracking. Install and manage Google Tag Manager and other marketing tools. Create thank you pages and lead funnels. Run A/B tests on headlines, layouts and calls to action. Analyse user behaviour using heatmaps and analytics software. Make weekly improvements based on performance data. Work alongside AI tools such as Claude and ChatGPT to rapidly implement ideas and marketing improvements. Essential Skills Strong WordPress experience. Strong Shopify experience. Elementor experience. Excellent understanding of conversion rate optimisation (CRO). Experience with Meta Pixel and Google Analytics 4. Understanding of user experience (UX). Mobile-first design experience. Basic HTML and CSS knowledge. Ability to troubleshoot technical issues independently. Bonus Skills Google Tag Manager. Microsoft Clarity or Hotjar. Copywriting for landing pages. Facebook Ads knowledge. Shopify apps and integrations. WooCommerce. SEO. What Success Looks Like We don't measure success by how attractive a website looks. We measure success by improvements in: Conversion rate Sales Leads generated Average order value Revenue Customer experience Every change should have a clear commercial reason. Who We're Looking For We want someone who enjoys solving problems and making websites perform better every week. You should be proactive, analytical and comfortable suggesting improvements rather than waiting to be told what to do. This is an opportunity to become a long-term member of a growing business with multiple brands and exciting projects. To Apply Please include: Examples of WordPress websites you've improved. Examples of Shopify stores you've worked on. Before-and-after conversion improvements (if available). Your favourite optimisation you've made and why it increased sales.

last_week_candidates.summary: GoHighLevel Specialist + Website+Video & Social Media Creator Remote · Full-Time · US Eye-Care Medical Group (Florida) Base $700–$1,100 USD/month + uncapped new-patient bonuses that can DOUBLE your base. Read This First This is not a generic VA job. We are...
last_week_candidates.full_description: GoHighLevel Specialist + Website+Video & Social Media Creator Remote · Full-Time · US Eye-Care Medical Group (Florida) Base $700–$1,100 USD/month + uncapped new-patient bonuses that can DOUBLE your base. Read This First This is not a generic VA job. We are hiring one exceptional person to run our marketing engine and grow it. We will train you, pay you on time, treat you like a teammate, and reward you directly every time your work brings in a new patient. The harder and smarter you work, the more you earn. Your base is just the floor. Your ceiling is set by you. If you are world-class at GoHighLevel AND can create scroll-stopping video and social content, keep reading. If you are a generalist who has “heard of” these tools, this is not your job — and that’s okay. The Offer (Why the Best Apply Here) Base salary $700 – $1,100 USD/month (full-time, 40 hrs/week) New-patient bonus $ USD for every qualifying new patient your work brings in (booked + shows up). No cap. Realistic earning Drive 35-50 new patients in a month and you will earn up to 50% of your base . The more you produce, the more you make — every month. Reviews Reviewed every 6 months. Strong performers reach the top of the base range within 90 days. 13th-month bonus Paid after 12 months. Paid time off Paid US holidays after probation + paid leave. Payment Bi-weekly via Wise — on time, every time. What the bonus means in plain numbers: $ per qualifying new patient, uncapped. A productive month can earn you more in bonuses than your entire base. We want you to out-earn the salary — that means we’re both winning. Schedule • 45-50 hrs/week (we may start you at 20–30 hrs/week part-time, with a clear, fast path to full-time). • Live overlap required: 9:00 AM – 200 PM US Eastern (9:00 PM – 1:00 AM Manila), Monday–Friday, for real-time work with the CEO and marketing lead. Some flexibility on exact hours. • The rest of your hours are flexible — we care about output, not clock-watching. About Us We are a US-based ophthalmology / eye-care medical group with three clinic locations in Florida. We treat thousands of patients every month, focused on senior eye health, diabetic eye disease, and advanced retinal care. We are stable, established, and real — not a fly-by-night agency. We have a small, dedicated in-house marketing team and an active marketing budget. You will work directly with the CEO and the marketing lead — no five layers of management between you and the people who make decisions and see your work. The Role — In One Sentence You will own our GoHighLevel (GHL) CRM end-to-end and produce the video and social content that brings senior and diabetic patients through our doors — then get paid a bonus on every patient you help bring in. What You'll Actually Do (Day-in-the-Life) — GoHighLevel Operations (the backbone) • Build and maintain workflows from scratch: patient recall, new-patient intake, no-show recovery, review requests, and our referral/ambassador program. • Manage and clean pipelines, opportunity stages, and contact tags. • Build SMS + email sequences (US-tone copy is supplied — you execute and polish, you don’t write from zero). • Set up and maintain calendar integrations and lead routing. • Build dashboards and weekly reports: leads in ? calls booked ? patients shown. • Connect GHL to forms, landing pages, and Meta lead forms. — Video & Social Media Creation (where you shine) • Create and edit scroll-stopping short-form video for YouTube, Instagram Reels, Facebook, and TikTok — content that actually gets seniors (65+) and diabetics to call us. • Run our organic social presence across all platforms, targeting patients within a 15–20 mile radius of each of our three offices. • Edit real footage of our doctors, staff, and (consented) patients into warm, trustworthy, human content. • Important: our audience is older adults and people managing a serious health condition. We use real people, real stories, and a caring tone — never AI avatars or AI voices. You’ll film, cut, caption, and publish content that feels like a trusted neighbor, not an ad. • Track what works (saves, shares, calls, DMs) and make more of it. — Paid Ads Support (Meta / Facebook) • Daily Ads Manager checks: spend, frequency, CPM, CTR, cost-per-lead. • Upload and launch creative; build and duplicate campaigns/ad sets. • Pull weekly performance reports. • You execute under direction — you are not the lead strategist — but you must know Meta Ads Manager cold, including the Special Ad Category (Health & Wellness) rules that apply to US healthcare advertisers. — Reporting + Documentation • Weekly Monday performance snapshot to the CEO. • Build and maintain an SOP document as you go, so the system never depends on one person. Must-Haves (Do Not Apply Without These) 1. 2+ years hands-on GoHighLevel — building workflows from scratch, not clicking templates. Be ready to show real workflows you’ve built. 2. Proven video editing + social content skills — short-form video for Reels / TikTok / YouTube. Be ready to show a portfolio or links to content you’ve made that performed. 3. Meta Ads and Google Manager experience — 1+ year managing live ad accounts. Bonus if you’ve worked under Meta’s Special Ad Category (Health & Wellness). 4. Excellent written and spoken English — you’ll write internal updates and US-facing SMS/email copy reviewed by the CEO. 5. Reliable internet (25 Mbps+), a quiet workspace, and a working webcam (weekly Zoom). 6. Long-term mindset — we’re hiring for years, not months. Job-hoppers will be cut early. Nice-to-Haves (Bonus Points) • US healthcare or medical-practice experience. • HIPAA awareness (we train, but knowing what patient data can’t touch is a plus). • Zapier / Make.com. • Canva / CapCut fluency for fast visual turnarounds. • Experience with patient recall, no-show recovery, or appointment-booking funnels. Who This Is Perfect For • You’ve run GHL for 2+ agencies and you’re tired of juggling 8 clients — you want one company, deep work, and real impact. • You can build systems and make a video that actually makes someone pick up the phone. • You’re motivated by results: you’d rather earn $ a patient with no cap than sit on a flat salary. • You want to grow into an Operations / Marketing Manager role within 12–18 months. • You want a US employer who pays on time, communicates clearly, and treats you like a teammate. Who This Is Not For • Generalist VAs who’ve only “heard of” GHL. • Agencies or teams reselling labor — we hire one human, directly. • Anyone unwilling to do a paid 2-hour test task before a final offer (you keep the pay even if not hired). How to Apply — Read Carefully (This Is Your First Test) We get hundreds of applications. This is how we filter for people who actually read and follow instructions. Subject line, exactly: CLEAR VISION — GHL + Video Specialist — [Your Name] In the body, include — in this order: 7. Codeword: “20/20” at the very top, so we know you read this. 8. 3 GoHighLevel workflows you personally built — 1–2 sentences each: the problem it solved and the result. 9. 2 video / social posts you created — links to the actual content, plus the result (views, saves, calls, leads). 10. 1 Meta Ads or Google campaign you ran — niche, monthly spend, what you optimized for, and the result. 11. Years of GHL experience + years of video/social experience + years of Meta Ads experience. 12. A Loom video (3 minutes max) walking through ONE workflow inside your own GHL account. Required — no Loom, no review. 13. Your full-time monthly rate in USD (and part-time hourly if relevant). 14. Earliest start date + how many hours of US Eastern overlap you can commit to. Applications missing any of the above are rejected automatically. Hiring Timeline • Applications reviewed daily. • Shortlist contacted within 5 business days. • Round 1: 30-min Zoom with the Marketing Lead. • Round 2: Paid 2-hour test task (you keep the pay even if not hired). • Final: 20-min call with the CEO. • Offer within 2 weeks of applying. Why You'll Want This Job • Stable US healthcare company — years in operation, not a startup that might vanish. • Real budget, real tools — you won’t be told to “make it work with free Mailchimp.” • Uncapped bonuses — your effort directly sets your income. Most months, your bonus can rival or beat your base. • Direct CEO access — your work is seen and rewarded. • Clear growth path — Operations / Marketing Manager role opening within 12–18 months for the right person. • One company, one mission, deep focus — no client-juggling. We are looking for one excellent person. Take the time to apply properly — and welcome to a role where the harder you work, the more you earn.

last_week_candidates.summary: About the Role We’re looking for a detail-obsessed, highly organized Virtual Assistant to be the operational backbone of Arise Financial Coaching. This is a behind-the-scenes, keep-everything-moving kind of role — the person who makes sure nothing falls...
last_week_candidates.full_description: About the Role We’re looking for a detail-obsessed, highly organized Virtual Assistant to be the operational backbone of Arise Financial Coaching. This is a behind-the-scenes, keep-everything-moving kind of role — the person who makes sure nothing falls through the cracks so our lead coach can stay focused on delivering life-changing results to clients. You will be working inside a fast-growing financial coaching business that handles sensitive client data every single day. Discretion, professionalism, and a genuine respect for client confidentiality are non-negotiable here. If you love systems, take pride in precision, and feel genuinely excited about the world of personal finance — this role was built for you. About the Company Arise Financial Coaching helps professional women break up with financial stress and anxiety for good. Founded by Nicole Stanley, Arise has supported hundreds of clients in paying off debt, saving more, and building wealth with automated money systems that work without restrictive budgets or willpower. Our flagship program, the Financial Goal Accelerator (FGA), takes clients through a three-phase coaching journey — from financial clarity all the way to a fully automated money system running in the background of their lives. We’ve been featured on Good Morning America and have a growing team of coaches, a thriving client community, and a whole lot of momentum. We move fast, we care deeply about the client experience, and we hold ourselves to a high standard. If you thrive in environments that are organized but dynamic, and you want to be part of a business that genuinely changes lives — you’ll fit right in here. Responsibilities Will Include Client Support & Operations Monitoring the client support inbox and triaging incoming tickets, emails, and requests Following up with clients on outstanding prep work, documents, and onboarding steps Tracking client progress through the FGA program and flagging anyone who appears stuck or behind Coordinating client onboarding and offboarding from start to finish using established task templates in ClickUp Processing failed payments and following up with clients on billing issues Ensuring all client records in Airtable are accurate, up to date, and fully populated Systems & Admin Managing and maintaining Airtable bases — updating records, running automations, and flagging errors Keeping ClickUp task templates active and on track across all client workflows Monitoring Zapier automations and flagging any failures or syncing issues Supporting the team with scheduling, documentation, and internal coordination Assisting with the setup and testing of Fillout forms and ThriveCart fulfillment Maintaining SOPs and keeping the operations manual up to date Team & Coach Support Acting as a communication bridge between the coaching team and clients where appropriate Supporting coaches with pre-session prep logistics — confirming client readiness, flagging outstanding documents Tracking weekly hours submissions from coaching team members Flagging last_week_candidates.summary: Job Title: AI & Business Growth Specialist Location: Remote Job Type: Part-Time | Long-Term Starting Rate: $5.00/hour (Performance & Revenue Review After 90 Days) Overview We are seeking a highly skilled, systems-oriented professional with expertise in AI...
last_week_candidates.full_description: Job Title: AI & Business Growth Specialist Location: Remote Job Type: Part-Time | Long-Term Starting Rate: $5.00/hour (Performance & Revenue Review After 90 Days) Overview We are seeking a highly skilled, systems-oriented professional with expertise in AI, automation, APIs, GoHighLevel (GHL), social media management, digital marketing, online marketplaces, and e-commerce operations. This role is responsible for designing, implementing, and managing the technology and marketing systems that power our growing digital business ecosystem. You will build intelligent workflows, optimize business operations, automate repetitive processes, manage social media platforms, and develop scalable systems that support customer acquisition, sales, and long-term business growth. We're looking for someone who enjoys solving complex problems, thinks in systems rather than tasks, embraces AI as a productivity multiplier, and is committed to delivering measurable business results. Key Responsibilities AI, Automation & Systems Development Design, build, and implement AI-powered workflows that improve operational efficiency across multiple businesses. Develop scalable business systems that integrate marketing, sales, customer service, operations, and internal processes. Create and maintain automation workflows using AI platforms, APIs, webhooks, Zapier, Make.com, GoHighLevel, and other integration tools. Build and optimize CRM systems in GoHighLevel, including: Pipelines Automations Lead nurturing Sales funnels Forms Calendars Customer journeys Integrate CRMs, e-commerce platforms, marketplaces, payment systems, communication tools, and other SaaS applications into a unified ecosystem. Design scalable system architecture that supports business expansion and operational efficiency. Analyze workflows to identify bottlenecks and replace manual processes with automation wherever possible. Troubleshoot automation issues and continuously improve system reliability, security, and performance. Maintain documentation, workflow diagrams, and Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) for all systems and automations. Research, test, and implement emerging AI technologies that improve productivity, customer experience, and operational performance. Social Media Management & Digital Marketing Manage multiple social media accounts across different brands while maintaining consistent branding and messaging. Develop and execute social media strategies that support business growth, customer engagement, and lead generation. Create, edit, schedule, and publish high-quality content using AI-assisted content creation tools. Design and maintain monthly content calendars aligned with marketing campaigns and promotional initiatives. Monitor engagement, audience growth, reach, and campaign performance across all platforms. Analyze social media analytics and provide actionable recommendations to improve visibility, conversions, and ROI. Respond to comments, messages, and customer inquiries when required while maintaining a professional brand voice. Repurpose content across multiple platforms to maximize reach and efficiency. Automate content scheduling, publishing, customer engagement, lead capture, and follow-up sequences using GoHighLevel and other automation platforms. Stay current on platform updates, social media trends, AI marketing tools, and emerging digital marketing strategies. E-Commerce & Marketplace Operations Support the development, optimization, and management of e-commerce stores and marketplace operations. Assist with product listings, inventory workflows, promotional campaigns, and customer experience improvements. Build systems that streamline marketplace operations and improve operational efficiency. Integrate marketplace platforms with CRM, automation, inventory, and marketing systems. Identify opportunities to improve conversion rates, customer acquisition, and operational scalability through automation and AI. Business Strategy & Process Improvement Collaborate with leadership to translate business objectives into scalable technology and marketing solutions. Recommend AI, automation, and digital marketing strategies that improve productivity and revenue. Ensure accurate data synchronization and seamless information flow across all connected platforms. Develop systems that improve customer acquisition, marketing performance, sales processes, reporting, and business intelligence. Continuously identify opportunities to optimize workflows, reduce operational costs, and improve overall business performance. Qualifications Required Strong knowledge of AI tools and practical AI implementation. Experience with automation platforms such as Zapier, Make.com, GoHighLevel, or similar. Experience working with APIs, webhooks, and third-party integrations. Hands-on experience with GoHighLevel (GHL). Experience managing business social media accounts and digital marketing campaigns. Understanding of major social media platforms, analytics, scheduling tools, and content strategies. Experience supporting e-commerce businesses or online marketplaces. Strong analytical, problem-solving, and systems-thinking abilities. Excellent organizational and time management skills. Ability to work independently while meeting deadlines. Strong written and verbal communication skills. Preferred Experience designing end-to-end business automation systems. Background in AI implementation or systems architecture. Experience integrating multiple SaaS platforms. Familiarity with Shopify, WooCommerce, Amazon, Walmart Marketplace, Etsy, or similar platforms. Experience with prompt engineering and AI-assisted content creation. Experience creating dashboards, reporting systems, and business analytics. Understanding of SEO, paid advertising, and digital marketing automation is a plus. Hiring Process Our hiring process focuses on demonstrated ability rather than interviews alone. The process includes: Application review Initial interview (for shortlisted candidates) Paid production-based trial assignment involving real business tasks representative of the role Evaluation based on: Quality of work Problem-solving ability Accuracy and attention to detail Timeliness Communication Initiative and ability to work independently Final hiring decision and onboarding Successful completion of the paid trial assignment is required before full onboarding. This process allows both you and our team to determine whether the position is the right long-term fit. What You Can Expect Starting rate of $5.00/hour Performance and revenue review after 90 days Opportunity for an increase to $6.00/hour based on performance and contribution Long-term remote position Growth opportunities as our businesses expand Opportunity to contribute to multiple companies within our digital mall ecosystem Exposure to cutting-edge AI technologies, automation systems, CRM development, digital marketing, and e-commerce operations Collaborative environment focused on innovation, efficiency, continuous improvement, and measurable business results About the Company We are building a multi-business digital ecosystem powered by AI, automation, scalable operational systems, digital marketing, and intelligent business processes. Our mission is to create an integrated infrastructure that connects multiple companies through automation, technology, and revenue-driven execution. We value professionals who are proactive, systems-oriented, resourceful, execution-focused, and committed to building long-term solutions that create measurable business impact. Application Link: forms.gle/n3MHgVGeinh3B2Zg9

last_week_candidates.summary: About the Role: We are looking for a highly skilled and proactive AI Specialist from the Philippines to join our team. We are currently developing custom AI solutions designed to support our district managers and leadership teams with daily operational...
last_week_candidates.full_description: About the Role: We are looking for a highly skilled and proactive AI Specialist from the Philippines to join our team. We are currently developing custom AI solutions designed to support our district managers and leadership teams with daily operational audits, policy compliance, and employee performance tracking. As we transition our initial AI prototypes into company-wide rollouts, we need an expert to help us scale these systems, automate day-to-day workflows, and seamlessly integrate AI tools across our various departments (including operations and marketing) and existing tech stack. Work Schedule: This is a remote position that requires working during standard business hours in Eastern Standard Time (EST). Key Responsibilities: AI Development & Scaling: Manage and refine existing conversational AI models, optimizing prompts to extract actionable insights from standard daily operational reports. Data Architecture & Integration: Architect and manage scalable cloud integrations (e.g., mapping custom models to a master corporate OneDrive) to bypass native AI file upload constraints and establish a unified knowledge base. Data Integrity & Maintenance: Continuously clean, structure, and update backend training data to eliminate AI hallucinations, ensuring all technical and operational guidance is 100?curate. Workflow & Document Automation: Develop automated systems that seamlessly format conversational AI outputs into compliant, exportable, print-ready documents (such as PDFs or Word files) directly from the interface. Privacy & Security Controls: Implement robust security protocols and access controls to isolate chat histories on shared workplace devices, safeguarding sensitive corporate data and personal information. Multi-Platform Integration & Programming: Evaluate and program integrations with alternative advanced AI engines (such as Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini, or others) and automation frameworks (like n8n) to continuously expand and upgrade our system's capabilities. Multimedia Expansion: Assist in building out dynamic resources, including mapping text-based troubleshooting prompts to external instructional video playlists. Required Skills & Qualifications: - Proven experience building, configuring, and scaling Custom GPTs and AI chatbots. - Strong understanding of the Microsoft 365 ecosystem (specifically OneDrive integration with AI platforms). - Experience exploring or building with alternative AI models like Anthropic Claude or Google Gemini. - Experience with prompt engineering methodologies (Context, Person, Results framework is a plus!). - Familiarity with workflow automation tools (e.g., n8n, Zapier, Make). - Ability to troubleshoot latency issues and optimize AI response times for fast-paced work environments. - Excellent problem-solving skills, particularly regarding data privacy, user access controls, and dynamic document generation. - Must be available to work dedicated hours in Eastern Standard Time (EST). To Apply: Please include examples of past AI bots or automation workflows you have built, particularly any projects involving Microsoft 365/OneDrive integrations, multi-platform AI use (e.g., Claude, Gemini), or dynamic document generation.

last_week_candidates.title: Backlink Outreach & Link Building Specialist - Base + Performance Bonus
last_week_candidates.summary: About CrowdReply CrowdReply is an AI brand visibility platform that helps brands see and improve how AI models like ChatGPT, Google AI Mode, Perplexity and Gemini talk about them. We're operating in one of the most exciting, fast-moving spaces in marketing...
last_week_candidates.full_description: About CrowdReply CrowdReply is an AI brand visibility platform that helps brands see and improve how AI models like ChatGPT, Google AI Mode, Perplexity and Gemini talk about them. We're operating in one of the most exciting, fast-moving spaces in marketing right now: AEO is still being defined, the playbook is being written in real time, and backlinks + brand mentions are becoming a core signal that feeds AI answers. Because the space is evolving so quickly, growth opportunities here are significant. You'll be joining early, taking real ownership, and helping build the link-building and brand-mention engine from the ground up with plenty of room to grow into a broader off-page/authority-building leadership role as we scale. About the Role We're looking for a Backlink Outreach Specialist to take full ownership of CrowdReply's link acquisition engine. Your single mission: consistently land high-quality backlinks and brand mentions that grow our domain authority and organic visibility. This is a hands-on, results-driven role. You'll own the entire outreach process from sourcing and qualifying prospects, to finding contacts, running email campaigns, and closing placements. You won't be writing on-site articles, doing technical audits, or handling on-page SEO. Your job is purely to build a strong, diverse, and clean backlink profile. We're a tech company with an in-house dev team, so if a part of your workflow can be automated, we'll build it for you. We'll also provide all the tools and access you need. What You'll Own - Campaign strategy & setup: Plan and run end-to-end link building campaigns with clear targets and timelines. - Prospecting & site sourcing: Identify high-quality, relevant, and authoritative websites using tools like Ahrefs and SEMrush (analyzing DR/DA, organic traffic, topical relevance, spam signals). - Contact discovery: Find and verify decision-maker contact details using Snov.io, Hunter, or similar tools. - Email outreach: Build, launch, and optimize outreach sequences on platforms like Lemlist, Instantly, or Smartlead — managing deliverability, follow-ups, and reply handling. - Link acquisition tactics: Execute across listicle outreach, guest post publishing, and niche edits. - Anchor text & diversification: Own anchor text strategy and maintain a natural, diversified, penalty-safe link profile. - Quality control: Identify and avoid toxic/spammy backlinks; prioritize high-authority, relevant placements. - Reporting: Track links landed, placement quality, reply rates, and campaign ROI. Must-Have Requirements - Proven experience running backlink outreach campaigns end-to-end with a track record of landed links. - Hands-on expertise with Ahrefs and/or SEMrush for prospecting and backlink analysis. - Experience with contact-finding tools (Snov.io, Hunter, or equivalents). - Experience with cold email / outreach platforms (Lemlist, Instantly, Smartlead, or similar), including deliverability best practices. - Strong grasp of anchor text strategy and link diversification. - Ability to distinguish toxic vs. high-quality backlinks. - Fluent with AI tools especially Claude — to speed up prospecting, personalization, filtering, and overall productivity. This is core to the role, not optional. - Excellent written English and self-management; able to take ownership with minimal supervision. Nice-to-Have - Familiarity with Parasite SEO — bonus if you've actually executed it. - Experience automating outreach workflows (n8n, Zapier, Make, scripts, etc.). - Existing relationships with publishers/site owners in relevant niches. What You WON'T Be Doing - Writing articles for our website - On-page / technical SEO or technical audits - Any on-site SEO work Your sole focus is acquiring high-quality backlinks and brand mentions for CrowdReply. What We Provide - All tools and access you need (Ahrefs, SEMrush, outreach platforms, contact finders, etc.) - Dev team support to automate parts of your workflow and maximize your output Performance based bonus details will be discussed once you're shortlisted When sending your application, answer this: What is 9+13?

last_week_candidates.title: Creative Strategist (Performance Marketing) [Singapore Company]
last_week_candidates.skill_requirement: Facebook Ads Ads Creation Creative Advertising
last_week_candidates.summary: Creative Strategist — KYOR (Performance Creative) 1) About Us Symple Wellness created KYOR, a science-backed wellness brand focused on clean formulations, clinically studied ingredients, and real results. Our hero product line is designed to simplify skincare...
last_week_candidates.full_description: Creative Strategist — KYOR (Performance Creative) 1) About Us Symple Wellness created KYOR, a science-backed wellness brand focused on clean formulations, clinically studied ingredients, and real results. Our hero product line is designed to simplify skincare and boost whole-body wellness — empowering individuals to glow confidently from the inside out. Website: kyor.co/ We are rapidly expanding, especially in the US market, and we're looking for a sharp, analytical, performance-driven **Creative Strategist** who lives and breathes paid social. This is the person who decides *what* we say, *who* we say it to, and *why* it'll convert — then writes the briefs that turn that thinking into ads our designers and editors can build. You won't be pushing pixels in Photoshop all day. Your output is the strategy and the brief: the angle, the persona, the hook, the concept, the shotlist. The visuals follow your thinking. This is one of the highest-leverage roles on the team — get the brief right and everything downstream scales; get it wrong and we burn spend on beautiful ads that don't sell. 2) What You'll Do - Write 20+ creative briefs per week that translate strategy into clear, buildable direction for designers and video editors across Meta (Feed, Reels, Stories), TikTok, and other platforms - Develop distinct messaging angles mapped to specific personas, pain points, and awareness stages — not "glow from within" mush, but sharp, differentiated concepts that filter for the right buyer - Research relentlessly: tear down competitor ads, build and maintain swipe files, study what's winning in beauty/wellness feeds, and reverse-engineer *why* it works - Define hooks, concepts, and formats for each brief — including the first 3 seconds, the scroll-stopper, and the CTA - Write UGC scripts, listicle scripts, and shotlists when a brief needs them, and spec AI image/video prompts clearly enough that a designer can execute without guessing - Partner with designers, copywriters, and our influencer/KOL team to ideate and pressure-test concepts before they go into production - Read the data and let it drive the next brief: hook rate, CTR, CPA, ROAS, thumbstop. Kill what's losing, double down on what's winning, and brief accordingly - Build and extend the frameworks, templates, and naming conventions that let the team move fast and stay organised 3) What You Bring **Must-haves** - 2+ year working on paid social creative — as a strategist, brief-writer, media buyer who briefs creative, UGC strategist, agency creative, or a strong self-directed track record (your own ads, teardowns, or a brand you've run creative for all count) - Strategic thinking with an actual framework — you reach for persona ? pain ? angle ? hook ? format, not vibes. When you watch an ad you can say *precisely* why it works or doesn't - Strong, clear writing. A brief is only useful if a designer can build from it without a meeting. You write tight, specific, and unambiguous - Working understanding of how paid social performs: what hook rate, CTR, CPA, and ROAS mean, and how creative moves them - The ability to generate volume, not just curate. Performance creative is a numbers game — you can produce 15 hooks fast, then know which 3 are worth shooting - Comfort taking direct, critical feedback and iterating without ego. The job is briefing creative against data feedback every single week, often when the data says your favourite concept flopped. You revise, you don't sulk - Hungry to learn, genuinely curious about why people buy, and energised by the idea that your work is measured by performance, not praise 4) AI Proficiency (required) We expect everyone on this team to use AI to think faster, research deeper, and produce more. You should already be comfortable with — or eager to learn — the tools we use day to day: - **Ideation, research & brief-writing:** ChatGPT, Claude, or similar — for brainstorming angles, structuring briefs, analysing reviews and competitor ads, and pressure-testing concepts - **Prompt engineering for production:** you can write image/video prompts precise enough for a designer to run through Nano Banana (Google Gemini's image model), ChatGPT Images, Seedance 2.0, or Veo 3 and get usable output - **Workflow:** you use AI to compress research and first drafts so you spend your time on the thinking that matters, not the busywork In the interview process, you'll be asked to show how AI actually shows up in your workflow — not that you've tried these tools, but how you've integrated them into how you strategise and write. If you haven't used our exact stack, that's fine — you should have hands-on experience with comparable tools and be ready to pick ours up quickly. **Nice-to-haves** - Personal interest in beauty, wellness, or skincare (you're a KYOR target customer or close to one) - Familiarity with supplement/beauty compliance — claim substantiation, on-label mechanism language, the difference between a sellable claim and a liability - Experience with Meta Ads Manager, creative testing, or structured ad scorecards - Hands-on UGC and influencer/KOL experience — scripting, shotlists, working with creators - An eye for art direction — you can't make the ad, but you know what good looks like and can point a designer toward it ## 5) How to Apply Send the following to teamsymple@gmail.com with the subject line: "I Am Your Creative Strategist — [Your Name]" - Your CV - A link to your work — a portfolio, swipe deck, ad teardown thread, briefs you've written, ads you've strategised, or a brand you've run creative for (Behance, Notion, Google Drive, PDF, or social all fine) - A 3–5 sentence note on which AI tools you currently use in your strategy/research workflow and how We respond to every application within 5 working days. Shortlisted candidates will go through a short three-round process: a 15-minute personality profiling assessment, followed by 2 strategy tests to understand how you think.

last_week_candidates.title: DevOps Engineer
last_week_candidates.summary: If your instinct when something breaks is to fix the pipeline before fixing the symptom — this role was built for you. To apply please fill out the application form here: u247.us/bepartofourteam. About the company We're a family-owned business with all-local...
last_week_candidates.full_description: If your instinct when something breaks is to fix the pipeline before fixing the symptom — this role was built for you. To apply please fill out the application form here: u247.us/bepartofourteam. About the company We're a family-owned business with all-local Philippines leadership and team. We've been 100% work-from-home since day one — not a pandemic pivot. We've grown by valuing our people, building long-term relationships, and doing great work for great clients. The Role You're not here to deploy code and move on. You're here to make sure the systems that deliver that code are reliable, secure, automated, and continuously improving. We're looking for a DevOps Engineer who thinks in pipelines, not tasks. You'll own the infrastructure that keeps our development and client environments running — CI/CD pipelines, Linux systems, cloud platforms, monitoring, and security. When something breaks, you find the root cause. When something works, you ask how to make it more reliable, more automated, and less dependent on human intervention next time. The Mindset We're Looking For The best DevOps engineers we've worked with share a few traits: They treat manual processes as technical debt. If something requires a human to do it repeatedly, their instinct is to eliminate that dependency — not just document it They take ownership. From the moment an infrastructure problem is theirs, it's theirs until it's resolved, documented, and hardened against happening again They think in systems. They don't just fix what's broken — they ask what else could break, and why, and build accordingly They communicate clearly. Status, blockers, and deployment risks are shared openly — before being asked for They keep learning. The DevOps landscape moves fast. Twelve months from now they know more than they do today — because they made sure of it Who Thrives Here People who are technically sharp, operationally minded, and genuinely excited by the challenge of building infrastructure that just works. You don't wait to be handed a list of things to automate — you find them. You're comfortable with ambiguity, resourceful under pressure, and you care more about system reliability than staying in a defined lane. We're a growing MSP, which means not every pipeline is optimized and not every process is documented yet. For the right engineer, that's not a warning — it's the opportunity to build things the right way from the ground up. What You'll Do You'll walk into an environment where development pipelines, Linux infrastructure, cloud systems, and client integrations all need an engineer who can own them end-to-end. Your job isn't just to keep things running — it's to make them run better, more reliably, and with less human intervention every month. That looks like: Designing, building, and maintaining CI/CD pipelines that make deployments fast, reliable, and repeatable — so shipping code is a non-event, not a risk Managing and administering Linux servers (Ubuntu, CentOS, Debian) across development and client environments — knowing these systems well enough to troubleshoot them at any layer Administering web servers (Nginx, Apache) and databases — keeping them performant, secure, and maintainable Implementing client-requested integrations — understanding what's being asked, translating it into a reliable technical solution, and delivering it cleanly Conducting root cause analysis on production errors — not just fixing what's visible, but understanding what allowed it to happen and building the safeguard that prevents it Building scripts and automation (Bash required; Python and PHP a plus) that eliminate repetitive manual work from the team's daily operations Monitoring infrastructure, resource utilization, and security events — building alerting systems that catch problems before they become incidents Designing and enforcing DevSecOps practices — making security a built-in part of the pipeline, not an afterthought Working with cloud platforms and virtualization — managing hypervisors and cloud service providers with the confidence of someone who's done it in production What We're Looking For Proven experience in Linux server administration (Ubuntu, CentOS, Debian) — you know these systems well enough to manage, troubleshoot, and optimize them without a guide Hands-on CI/CD experience — you've built and maintained real pipelines, not just used them; you know what makes a deployment safe and what makes it a liability Strong version control administration — Git, remote repositories, branching strategies, code review workflows; you manage these with intention, not just habit Web server administration experience — Nginx and Apache, configuration, performance tuning, and troubleshooting Database administration experience — you can manage database servers, optimize queries, and keep data environments healthy Bash scripting proficiency (required) — you write automation that works, that others can read, and that makes the team faster; Python and PHP experience is a plus Solid cloud computing and virtualization experience — working with hypervisors and cloud service providers (AWS, GCP, Azure, or equivalent) in real production environments Infrastructure and event monitoring experience — you build alerting systems that surface the right information at the right time, not noise Understanding of DevSecOps — you treat security as part of the build, not something bolted on at the end Strong diagnostic skills — you find the root cause of a production issue, not just the fastest path to closing the ticket Self-directed once you understand the environment — but you ask smart questions early to get there Honest communicator — you surface risks and blockers clearly and early, even when the news isn't good Long-term mindset — you want to build something that lasts, not move on when things get complex How Onboarding Works (And How That Changes) We're honest about how we work with new team members. In your first weeks and months, you'll have real hands-on support from the team. We'll work closely with you to understand our infrastructure, our standards, our tools, and how we operate. We expect questions — and we'll give you the time to learn. As you show what you can do — and you will — that involvement steps back quickly. Trust grows fast here. People who demonstrate ownership, judgment, and quality work earn autonomy quickly. People who consistently deliver end up with significant latitude to shape the infrastructure, the pipelines, and how we build. We don't want to over-direct people who don't need it. But we also don't believe in throwing someone in without context and calling it autonomy. We earn each other's trust through real work — then we get out of the way. What Success Looks Like At three months: You have a clear picture of the infrastructure landscape — what's healthy, what's fragile, and what needs to be rebuilt. At least one significant manual process has been automated. Your documentation is already what the team references. At six months: Deployments are more reliable and less stressful than when you joined. Monitoring is catching problems before they become incidents. The team is spending less time on reactive firefighting and more time building. At twelve months: The infrastructure is measurably more stable, secure, and automated than before you joined. You've built systems the team depends on — and you're already identifying the next layer of improvement. The company is investing in your next step. How We Work We trust our people to do great work and we give them the space to do it. There's real work to be done — clients and internal teams depend on the infrastructure you manage — but we understand that doing meaningful work means making decisions, and sometimes those decisions won't be right on the first try. That's how we find the right path. We don't punish thoughtful mistakes. We learn from them and move forward. We're a growing MSP, so not every pipeline is optimized and not every process is documented yet. That's part of why we need someone like you — to help us build the infrastructure, automations, and systems that make us better as we grow. This Role Is Not for You If: You need a defined list of tasks to know what to work on You deploy the fix without asking what caused the failure You treat documentation and runbooks as someone else's job You're comfortable with fragile systems as long as they're currently running Your outside commitments regularly affect your availability and focus You're looking for a low-pressure remote role with predictable, well-defined work There's no judgment in that list — it's just not the environment. We'd rather be honest now than waste each other's time. What We Offer Competitive salary based on experience 100% remote — no corporate commute, ever Weekly pay Tech and internet allowance Paid vacation leave HMO Access to the latest AI tools (Claude, Code, and more) to work smarter and automate more Career growth

last_week_candidates.full_description: Executive Assistant / Chief of Staff to Real Estate Entrepreneur This is a fairly long job post requiring careful reading, and there are many questions to answer as part of this process. It may take some time but it could very well be worth it because we are urgently hiring an awesome person to join our excellent team immediately! We offer excellent compensation for the right people. We are ready to hire the right individual immediately. We are a growing real estate investment, property management, and title company organization based in Miami, Florida. Our companies own and manage over 100 residential rental units, including multifamily, single-family rentals, Section 8 housing, and short-term rental properties. We also operate a successful title company and are actively acquiring additional properties. We are looking for an exceptional Executive Assistant / Chief of Staff who can become a long-term strategic partner to the founder. This is not an entry-level assistant position. You will help organize, manage, and improve operations across multiple businesses while helping the founder stay focused on the highest-value activities. We are looking for someone who enjoys solving problems, taking ownership, creating systems, and making things happen without constant supervision. The Right Person is: Extremely organized Highly intelligent Detail-oriented Resourceful Trustworthy Proactive Comfortable figuring things out independently Excellent at written and verbal communication Calm under pressure Able to manage many projects simultaneously You don't wait for instructions. You identify problems and bring solutions. You follow through relentlessly until tasks are completed. You double-check your work before submitting it. You think like an owner. Responsibilities: Executive Support Calendar management Meeting coordination Travel planning Event coordination Inbox management Follow-up management Vendor coordination Personal scheduling Real Estate Operations Track acquisitions and due diligence Coordinate inspections Software implementation Track contract deadlines Coordinate lenders, attorneys, brokers, title companies, and other real estate vendors Maintain deal pipeline reports Assist with underwriting and research Prepare meeting materials Property Management Support Monitor property management KPIs Track vacancies Track leasing performance Coordinate maintenance vendors Follow up on tenant issues Improve operating procedures Help build and document systems Project Management Assistance with health and wellness matters Manage special projects from start to finish Track deliverables and deadlines Create accountability systems Build SOPs and workflows Market research Vendor research Property research Competitive analysis AI tools and automation research Problem-solving assignments Draft emails Prepare reports Communicate with vendors Coordinate internal teams Follow up until completion Required Skills: Exceptional written English Strong business communication skills Excellent organizational ability Advanced Google Workspace skills Strong Microsoft Office skills Project management experience Ability to learn new software quickly Strong internet research skills Bonus Qualifications: Experience with: Real estate investing Property management Section 8 housing Multifamily operations Airbnb / short-term rentals Title companies Monday.com ClickUp Asana Airtable Notion ChatGPT and AI tools EOS operating system What Success Looks Like Within 90 days: Founder inbox under control Calendars fully organized Critical tasks never missed Project tracking system implemented Weekly executive dashboard created Follow-up system running consistently SOP library started Team accountability improved Within 12 months: Become one of the founder's trusted inner circle individuals Manage major projects independently Help scale operations Reduce founder workload significantly Improve execution across all companies Compensation: Based on experience and capability. We are willing to pay significantly more for exceptional talent who can truly own outcomes and become a long-term partner. Application Questions: To be considered for this position, please answer all of the following questions in corresponding numbered answers and in detail. Generic or AI-generated answers will not be considered. Important: To confirm that you carefully read this entire job posting, begin your application with the words "Blue Mango." 1. Employment History How many employers have you worked for during the last 10 years? For each employer, please provide: Company name Job title Employment dates Reason for leaving What was the longest position you have ever held? 2. Executive Support Experience Describe the most demanding executive, entrepreneur, or business owner you have ever supported. Please include: Industry Company size Number of employees Number of projects managed simultaneously What made the role challenging 3. Problem-Solving Tell us about a major problem you identified before your manager, executive, or business owner became aware of it. What was the problem? What actions did you take? What was the outcome? 4. Last 3 Positions Why did you leave your last three positions? If you were terminated why? And if you resigned what could those employers have done to keep you longer? 5.. High-Pressure Work Environment Describe the busiest period of your career. Approximately how many projects, clients, properties, team members, or deadlines were you responsible for simultaneously? How did you stay organized? 6. Technology & Software Experience List every major software platform you have used professionally during the last three years. For each platform, rate your proficiency from 1–10. 7. Artificial Intelligence Experience How do you currently use AI tools in your work? Please provide at least three specific examples. 8.. Real Estate Experience Describe any experience you have with: Real estate investing Property management Multifamily housing Section 8 housing Construction or renovations Real estate acquisitions Lending Title companies Airbnb and short term rental properties Real estate law Please provide specific examples. 9.. Difficult People Describe the most difficult person you have ever had to work with. How did you handle the situation? What was the outcome? 10. Career Goals Where do you see yourself in three years? Where do you see yourself in five years? What would make you stay with one company for 10+ years? 11. Ownership Mentality Tell us about a situation where you inherited something that was disorganized, broken, inefficient, or failing. What specifically did you do to improve it? What measurable results did you achieve? 12. Compensation What annual compensation are you seeking? What compensation range would make you excited to stay with a company long term? 13. Availability How many hours per week are you currently available? What is your time zone? Are you willing to work U.S. Eastern Time business hours? 14. Inbox Management Test How many unread emails are currently in your inbox? What system do you personally use to ensure important items never fall through the cracks? 15. Data Have you ever had access to company bank accounts, financial information, confidential legal matters, passwords, payroll data, or sensitive business information? If so, describe your responsibilities. 16. Processes/Systems Describe a time you built a process or system that significantly improved operations. And do you have experience creating SOP's or implementing any operating procedures within an organization? 17. Term Why are you interested in a long-term position rather than freelancing? 18. EA Experience What direct experience have you had as an Executive Assistant?

last_week_candidates.summary: ABOUT THE ROLE We're building out our organic growth engine — affiliate partnerships, citation acquisition, and AI-assisted content — and we need someone to drive this work at scale. This role lives in research, spreadsheets, outreach databases, and analytics...
last_week_candidates.full_description: ABOUT THE ROLE We're building out our organic growth engine — affiliate partnerships, citation acquisition, and AI-assisted content — and we need someone to drive this work at scale. This role lives in research, spreadsheets, outreach databases, and analytics tools. You'll be prospecting YouTube creators and travel bloggers, building relationships through Commission Junction, finding citation and backlink opportunities, and eventually helping shape our AI content workflow. This is not a 'post on social media' marketing role. It's data-heavy, research-intensive, and requires someone who can sit with a project for days, build systems around it, and execute thousands of outreach touchpoints methodically. WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING Affiliate Network Growth (YouTube + Travel Blogs) • Research YouTube channels in the RV rentals, RV ownership, and travel/tourism space • Research and outreach to campgrounds and RV parks to expose and promote the brand • Identify contact information (typically in About sections) and build a structured prospect database • Reach out using approved scripts to invite creators to join our affiliate program via Commission Junction (CJ) • Apply the same research and outreach methodology to travel blogs and niche websites • Track outreach-to-conversion rates in CJ and optimize the approach over time Citation & Backlink Acquisition • Identify websites that mention competitors or geo-relevant RV content but don't yet reference our brands. • Evaluate domain authority and relevance using SEMrush and other SEO research tools • Reach out to site owners with content snippets or requests for new pages — building us up as an authority source for LLMs and search engines • Maintain a database of acquired citations and track progress over time AI Content Support • Assist in developing prompt templates for AI-generated content (blog posts, web pages, product descriptions) • Generate drafts using AI tools based on approved brand voice guidelines and structured prompts • Coordinate fact-checking with internal subject matter experts before content is published TOOLS YOU'LL WORK WITH • SEMrush (or equivalent) for keyword research, competitive gap analysis, and backlink research • Commission Junction for affiliate program management • ChatGPT / Claude or similar AI tools for research assistance and content drafting • Google Sheets / Airtable for prospect databases and outreach tracking • Email outreach tools for scaled communication YOU'RE A GREAT FIT IF YOU... • Are analytical, patient, and comfortable with repetitive, data-heavy work • Have 1–3 years of experience in SEO, digital marketing, content outreach, or affiliate marketing • Can work independently and build processes — not just follow them • Understand what 'domain authority' means and how organic search works • Communicate clearly in English and can represent us professionally in outreach • Are comfortable using AI tools as a productivity layer, not a crutch POTENTIAL TO GROW INTO • Full ownership of our content production pipeline • Managing external freelancers for content and SEO work • Strategic SEO planning alongside our existing agency

last_week_candidates.summary: ## Job Title: **SEO Specialist with Google Ads & Web Design Experience** ## Job Description Digital NinjaZ is a U.S.-based IT and digital marketing company located in Florida. We help small businesses with managed IT, cybersecurity, websites, SEO, Google Ads...
last_week_candidates.full_description: ## Job Title: **SEO Specialist with Google Ads & Web Design Experience** ## Job Description Digital NinjaZ is a U.S.-based IT and digital marketing company located in Florida. We help small businesses with managed IT, cybersecurity, websites, SEO, Google Ads, CCTV/security cameras, networking, and general technology support. We are looking for a reliable **SEO Specialist** who can help with SEO, backlink research, Google Ads support, website updates, and basic web design tasks. This position is remote, but some work may require logging into a secure computer located in our Florida office using remote desktop software. You must have **strong English reading and writing skills**. You need to understand written instructions, read client website content, write clear SEO content, and communicate with our U.S. team without constant clarification. Good spoken English is preferred for training calls and occasional meetings. We are also looking for someone who is comfortable using **AI tools like ChatGPT** to improve speed, research, content writing, SEO planning, and reporting. ## Responsibilities * Perform on-page SEO updates * Research keywords for local service businesses * Help write and improve website content * Create or update service pages * Optimize page titles, meta descriptions, headings, and image alt text * Assist with backlink research and outreach * Build and clean up local citations/directories * Help manage Google Business Profile optimization * Assist with Google Ads campaigns * Review Google Search Console and Google Analytics data * Help update WordPress, Wix, or other small business websites * Make basic web design edits * Prepare simple SEO reports * Use AI tools to speed up research, writing, and optimization * Track completed work and send daily updates ## Required Skills * SEO experience * Strong English reading and writing skills * Basic Google Ads knowledge * WordPress or Wix experience * Basic web design skills * Ability to follow detailed written instructions * Comfortable using AI tools like ChatGPT * Organized and detail-oriented * Reliable internet connection * Comfortable using remote desktop software * Available for some overlap with Florida Eastern Time ## Preferred Skills * Local SEO experience * Google Business Profile optimization * Backlink outreach * Citation building * Canva or basic graphic design * HTML/CSS basics * Experience with small business websites * Experience with tools like Google Analytics, Google Search Console, Ahrefs, SEMrush, Ubersuggest, Screaming Frog, or similar tools ## Work Schedule We prefer someone who can work **6–8 hours per day**, Monday to Friday. Some overlap with **Florida Eastern Time, USA** is required for communication, training, and task review. Occasional voice or video calls may be required during Florida business hours. ## Pay Pay depends on experience. Please include your requested hourly rate or monthly salary when applying. We prefer to start with a **paid trial period** before offering a long-term position. ## Important Requirements Please do not apply if you are not comfortable with: * Strong English reading and writing * Using remote desktop software * Using AI tools as part of your workflow * Tracking your completed work * Sending daily work summaries * Joining occasional voice or video calls * Starting with a paid trial period * Following step-by-step instructions ## How to Apply Please answer the following questions in English: 1. Tell us about your SEO experience. 2. Send examples of websites you have worked on. 3. What SEO tools have you used? 4. What Google Ads experience do you have? 5. What website platforms have you worked with? WordPress, Wix, Shopify, etc. 6. What AI tools do you use? 7. Are you comfortable using remote desktop software? 8. Are you comfortable joining occasional voice or video calls in English? 9. What hours can you work in Florida Eastern Time? 10. What is your requested hourly rate or monthly salary? 11. What are the first 5 SEO tasks you would perform for a local service business website? Use this subject line when applying: **SEO Specialist - Digital NinjaZ** Applications with short, copy-pasted, or poorly written answers will be ignored.
